avyayaḥ puruṣaḥ sākṣī kṣetrajño 'kṣara eva ca. (2)

slokas.devanagari:
योगो योगविदां नेता प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः ।
नारसिंहवपुः श्रीमान् केशवः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥३॥
slokas.roman:
yogo yogavidāṃ netā pPPradhānapuruṣeśvaraḥ,
nārasiṃhavapuḥ śrīmān keśavaḥ puruṣottamaḥ. (3)
slokas.text:
योगो योगविदां नेता प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः ।
नारसिंहवपुः श्रीमान् केशवः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥३॥

yogo yogavidāṃ netā pPPradhānapuruṣeśvaraḥ,
nārasiṃhavapuḥ śrīmān keśavaḥ puruṣottamaḥ. (3)

slokas.devanagari:
सर्वः शर्वः शिवः स्थाणुर्भूतादिर्निधिरव्ययः ।
सम्भवो भावनो भर्ता प्रभवः प्रभुरीश्वरः ॥४॥
slokas.roman:
sarvaḥ śarvaḥ śivaḥ sthāṇur bhūtādir nidhir avyayaḥ,
saṃbhavo bhāvano bhartā prabhavaḥ prabhur īśvaraḥ. (4)
slokas.text:
सर्वः शर्वः शिवः स्थाणुर्भूतादिर्निधिरव्ययः ।
सम्भवो भावनो भर्ता प्रभवः प्रभुरीश्वरः ॥४॥

sarvaḥ śarvaḥ śivaḥ sthāṇur bhūtādir nidhir avyayaḥ,
saṃbhavo bhāvano bhartā prabhavaḥ prabhur īśvaraḥ. (4)

slokas.devanagari:
स्वयम्भूः शम्भुरादित्यः पुष्कराक्षो महास्वनः ।
अनादिनिधनो धाता विधाता धातुरुत्तमः ॥५॥
slokas.roman:
svayaṃbhūḥ śambhur ādityaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahāsvanaḥ,
anādinidhano dhātā vidhātā dDDhāturuttamaḥ. (5)
slokas.text:
स्वयम्भूः शम्भुरादित्यः पुष्कराक्षो महास्वनः ।
अनादिनिधनो धाता विधाता धातुरुत्तमः ॥५॥

svayaṃbhūḥ śambhur ādityaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahāsvanaḥ,
anādinidhano dhātā vidhātā dDDhāturuttamaḥ. (5)

slokas.devanagari:
अप्रमेयो हृषीकेशः पद्मनाभोऽमरप्रभुः ।
विश्वकर्मा मनुस्त्वष्टा स्थविष्ठः स्थविरो ध्रुवः ॥६॥
slokas.roman:
aprameyo hṛṣīkeśaḥ padmanābho 'maraprabhuḥ,
viśvakarmā manus tvaṣṭā sthaviṣṭhaḥ sthaviro dhruvaḥ. (6)
slokas.text:
अप्रमेयो हृषीकेशः पद्मनाभोऽमरप्रभुः ।
विश्वकर्मा मनुस्त्वष्टा स्थविष्ठः स्थविरो ध्रुवः ॥६॥

aprameyo hṛṣīkeśaḥ padmanābho 'maraprabhuḥ,
viśvakarmā manus tvaṣṭā sthaviṣṭhaḥ sthaviro dhruvaḥ. (6)

slokas.devanagari:
अग्राह्यः शाश्वतः कृष्णो लोहिताक्षः प्रतर्दनः ।
प्रभूतस्त्रिककुब्धाम पवित्रं मङ्गलं परम् ॥७॥
slokas.roman:
agrāhyaḥ śāśvataḥ kṛṣṇo lohitākṣaḥ pratardanaḥ,
prabhūtas trikakubdhāma pavitraṃ maṅgalaṃ param. (7)
slokas.text:
अग्राह्यः शाश्वतः कृष्णो लोहिताक्षः प्रतर्दनः ।
प्रभूतस्त्रिककुब्धाम पवित्रं मङ्गलं परम् ॥७॥

agrāhyaḥ śāśvataḥ kṛṣṇo lohitākṣaḥ pratardanaḥ,
prabhūtas trikakubdhāma pavitraṃ maṅgalaṃ param. (7)

slokas.devanagari:
ईशानः प्राणदः प्राणो ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः प्रजापतिः ।
हिरण्यगर्भो भूगर्भो माधवो मधुसूदनः ॥८॥
slokas.roman:
īśānaḥ prāṇadaḥ prāṇo jyeṣṭhaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ pPPrajāpatiḥ,
hiraṇyagarbho bhūgarbho mādhavo madhusūdanaḥ. (8)
slokas.text:
ईशानः प्राणदः प्राणो ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः प्रजापतिः ।
हिरण्यगर्भो भूगर्भो माधवो मधुसूदनः ॥८॥

īśānaḥ prāṇadaḥ prāṇo jyeṣṭhaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ pPPrajāpatiḥ,
hiraṇyagarbho bhūgarbho mādhavo madhusūdanaḥ. (8)

slokas.devanagari:
ईश्वरो विक्रमी धन्वी मेधावी विक्रमः क्रमः ।
अनुत्तमो दुराधर्षः कृतज्ञः कृतिरात्मवान् ॥९॥
slokas.roman:
īśvaro vikramī dhanvī medhāvī vikramaḥ kramaḥ,
anuttamo durādharṣaḥ kṛtajñaḥ kṛtir ātmavān. (9)
slokas.text:
ईश्वरो विक्रमी धन्वी मेधावी विक्रमः क्रमः ।
अनुत्तमो दुराधर्षः कृतज्ञः कृतिरात्मवान् ॥९॥

īśvaro vikramī dhanvī medhāvī vikramaḥ kramaḥ,
anuttamo durādharṣaḥ kṛtajñaḥ kṛtir ātmavān. (9)

slokas.devanagari:
सुरेशः शरणं शर्म विश्वरेताः प्रजाभवः ।
अहः संवत्सरो व्यालः प्रत्ययः सर्वदर्शनः ॥१०॥
slokas.roman:
sureśaḥ śaraṇaṃ śarma viśvaretāḥ prajābhavaḥ,
ahaḥ saṃvatsaro vyālaḥ pratyayaḥ sarvadarśanaḥ. (10)
slokas.text:
सुरेशः शरणं शर्म विश्वरेताः प्रजाभवः ।
अहः संवत्सरो व्यालः प्रत्ययः सर्वदर्शनः ॥१०॥

sureśaḥ śaraṇaṃ śarma viśvaretāḥ prajābhavaḥ,
ahaḥ saṃvatsaro vyālaḥ pratyayaḥ sarvadarśanaḥ. (10)

slokas.devanagari:
अजः सर्वेश्वरः सिद्धः सिद्धिः सर्वादिरच्युतः ।
वृषाकपिरमेयात्मा सर्वयोगविनिःसृतः ॥११॥
slokas.roman:
ajaḥ sarveśvaraḥ siddhaḥ siddhiḥ sarvādy acyutaḥ,
vṛṣākapir ameyātmā sarvayogaviniḥsṛtaḥ. (11)
slokas.text:
अजः सर्वेश्वरः सिद्धः सिद्धिः सर्वादिरच्युतः ।
वृषाकपिरमेयात्मा सर्वयोगविनिःसृतः ॥११॥

ajaḥ sarveśvaraḥ siddhaḥ siddhiḥ sarvādy acyutaḥ,
vṛṣākapir ameyātmā sarvayogaviniḥsṛtaḥ. (11)

slokas.devanagari:
वसुर्वसुमनाः सत्यः समात्माऽसम्मितः समः ।
अमोघः पुण्डरीकाक्षो वृषकर्मा वृषाकृतिः ॥१२॥
slokas.roman:
vasur vasumanāḥ satyaḥ samātmā sammitaḥ samaḥ,
amoghaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣo vṛṣakarmā vṛṣākṛtiḥ. (12)
slokas.text:
वसुर्वसुमनाः सत्यः समात्माऽसम्मितः समः ।
अमोघः पुण्डरीकाक्षो वृषकर्मा वृषाकृतिः ॥१२॥

vasur vasumanāḥ satyaḥ samātmā sammitaḥ samaḥ,
amoghaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣo vṛṣakarmā vṛṣākṛtiḥ. (12)

slokas.devanagari:
रुद्रो बहुशिरा बभ्रुर्विश्वयोनिः शुचिश्रवाः ।
अमृतः शाश्वतस्थाणुर्वरारोहो महातपाः ॥१३॥
slokas.roman:
rudro bahuśirā babhrur viśvayoniḥ śuciśravāḥ,
amṛtaḥ śāśvatasthāṇur varāroho mahātapāḥ. (13)
slokas.text:
रुद्रो बहुशिरा बभ्रुर्विश्वयोनिः शुचिश्रवाः ।
अमृतः शाश्वतस्थाणुर्वरारोहो महातपाः ॥१३॥

rudro bahuśirā babhrur viśvayoniḥ śuciśravāḥ,
amṛtaḥ śāśvatasthāṇur varāroho mahātapāḥ. (13)

slokas.devanagari:
सर्वगः सर्वविद्भानुर्विष्वक्सेनो जनार्दनः ।
वेदो वेदविदव्यङ्गो वेदाङ्गो वेदवित् कविः ॥१४॥
slokas.roman:
sarvagaḥ sarvavid bhānur viṣvakṣeno janārdanaḥ,
vedo vedavid avyaṅgo vedāṅgo vedavit kaviḥ. (14)
slokas.text:
सर्वगः सर्वविद्भानुर्विष्वक्सेनो जनार्दनः ।
वेदो वेदविदव्यङ्गो वेदाङ्गो वेदवित् कविः ॥१४॥

sarvagaḥ sarvavid bhānur viṣvakṣeno janārdanaḥ,
vedo vedavid avyaṅgo vedāṅgo vedavit kaviḥ. (14)

slokas.devanagari:
लोकाध्यक्षः सुराध्यक्षो धर्माध्यक्षः कृताकृतः ।
चतुरात्मा चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्दंष्ट्रश्चतुर्भुजः ॥१५॥
slokas.roman:
lokādhyakṣaḥ surādhyakṣo dharmādhyakṣaḥ kṛtākṛtaḥ,
caturātmā caturvyūhaś caturdaṃṣṭraś caturbhujaḥ. (15)
slokas.text:
लोकाध्यक्षः सुराध्यक्षो धर्माध्यक्षः कृताकृतः ।
चतुरात्मा चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्दंष्ट्रश्चतुर्भुजः ॥१५॥

lokādhyakṣaḥ surādhyakṣo dharmādhyakṣaḥ kṛtākṛtaḥ,
caturātmā caturvyūhaś caturdaṃṣṭraś caturbhujaḥ. (15)

slokas.devanagari:
भ्राजिष्णुर्भोजनं भोक्ता सहिष्णुर्जगदादिजः ।
अनघो विजयो जेता विश्वयोनिः पुनर्वसुः ॥१६॥
slokas.roman:
bhrājiṣṇur bhojanaṃ bhoktā sahiṣṇur jagadādijaḥ,
anagho vijayo jetā viśvayoniḥ punarvasuḥ. (16)
slokas.text:
भ्राजिष्णुर्भोजनं भोक्ता सहिष्णुर्जगदादिजः ।
अनघो विजयो जेता विश्वयोनिः पुनर्वसुः ॥१६॥

bhrājiṣṇur bhojanaṃ bhoktā sahiṣṇur jagadādijaḥ,
anagho vijayo jetā viśvayoniḥ punarvasuḥ. (16)

slokas.devanagari:
उपेन्द्रो वामनः प्रांशुरमोघः शुचिरूर्जितः ।
अतीन्द्रः सङ्ग्रहः सर्गो धृतात्मा नियमो यमः ॥१७॥
slokas.roman:
upendro vāmanaḥ prāṃśur amoghaḥ śucir ūrjitaḥ,
atīndraḥ saṅgrahaḥ sargo dhṛtātmā niyamo yamaḥ. (17)
slokas.text:
उपेन्द्रो वामनः प्रांशुरमोघः शुचिरूर्जितः ।
अतीन्द्रः सङ्ग्रहः सर्गो धृतात्मा नियमो यमः ॥१७॥

upendro vāmanaḥ prāṃśur amoghaḥ śucir ūrjitaḥ,
atīndraḥ saṅgrahaḥ sargo dhṛtātmā niyamo yamaḥ. (17)

slokas.devanagari:
वेद्यो वैद्यः सदायोगी वीरहा माधवो मधुः ।
अतीन्द्रियो महामायो महोत्साहो महाबलः ॥१८॥
slokas.roman:
vedyo vaidyaḥ sadāyogī vīrahā mādhavo madhuḥ,
atīndriyo mahāmāyo mahotsāho mahābalaḥ. (18)
slokas.text:
वेद्यो वैद्यः सदायोगी वीरहा माधवो मधुः ।
अतीन्द्रियो महामायो महोत्साहो महाबलः ॥१८॥

vedyo vaidyaḥ sadāyogī vīrahā mādhavo madhuḥ,
atīndriyo mahāmāyo mahotsāho mahābalaḥ. (18)

slokas.devanagari:
महाबुद्धिर्महावीर्यो महाशक्तिर्महाद्युतिः ।
अनिर्देश्यवपुः श्रीमानमेयात्मा महाद्रिधृक् ॥१९॥
slokas.roman:
mahābuddhir mahāvīryo mahāśaktir mahādyutiḥ,
anirdeśyavapuḥ śrīmān ameyātmā mahādridhṛk. (19)
slokas.text:
महाबुद्धिर्महावीर्यो महाशक्तिर्महाद्युतिः ।
अनिर्देश्यवपुः श्रीमानमेयात्मा महाद्रिधृक् ॥१९॥

mahābuddhir mahāvīryo mahāśaktir mahādyutiḥ,
anirdeśyavapuḥ śrīmān ameyātmā mahādridhṛk. (19)

slokas.devanagari:
महेष्वासो महीभर्ता श्रीनिवासः सतां गतिः ।
अनिरुद्धः सुरानन्दो गोविन्दो गोविदां पतिः ॥२०॥
slokas.roman:
maheṣvāso mahībhartā śrīnivāsaḥ satāṃ gatiḥ,
aniruddhaḥ surānando govindo govidāṃ patiḥ. (20)
slokas.text:
महेष्वासो महीभर्ता श्रीनिवासः सतां गतिः ।
अनिरुद्धः सुरानन्दो गोविन्दो गोविदां पतिः ॥२०॥

maheṣvāso mahībhartā śrīnivāsaḥ satāṃ gatiḥ,
aniruddhaḥ surānando govindo govidāṃ patiḥ. (20)

slokas.devanagari:
मरीचिर्दमनो हंसः सुपर्णो भुजगोत्तमः ।
हिरण्यनाभः सुतपाः पद्मनाभः प्रजापतिः ॥२१॥
slokas.roman:
marīcir damano haṃsaḥ suparṇo bhujagottamaḥ,
hiraṇyanābhaḥ sutapāḥ padmanābhaḥ pPPrajāpatiḥ. (21)
slokas.text:
मरीचिर्दमनो हंसः सुपर्णो भुजगोत्तमः ।
हिरण्यनाभः सुतपाः पद्मनाभः प्रजापतिः ॥२१॥

marīcir damano haṃsaḥ suparṇo bhujagottamaḥ,
hiraṇyanābhaḥ sutapāḥ padmanābhaḥ pPPrajāpatiḥ. (21)

slokas.devanagari:
अमृत्युः सर्वदृक् सिंहः सन्धाता सन्धिमान् स्थिरः ।
अजो दुर्मर्षणः शास्ता विश्रुतात्मा सुरारिहा ॥२२॥
slokas.roman:
amṛtyuḥ sarvadṛk siṃhaḥ sandhātā sandhimān sthiraḥ,
ajo durmarṣaṇaḥ śāstā viśrutātmā surārihā. (22)
slokas.text:
अमृत्युः सर्वदृक् सिंहः सन्धाता सन्धिमान् स्थिरः ।
अजो दुर्मर्षणः शास्ता विश्रुतात्मा सुरारिहा ॥२२॥

amṛtyuḥ sarvadṛk siṃhaḥ sandhātā sandhimān sthiraḥ,
ajo durmarṣaṇaḥ śāstā viśrutātmā surārihā. (22)

slokas.devanagari:
गुरुर्गुरुतमो धाम सत्यः सत्यपराक्रमः ।
निमिषोऽनिमिषः स्रग्वी वाचस्पतिरुदारधीः ॥२३॥
slokas.roman:
gurur gurutamo dhāma satyaḥ satyaparākramaḥ,
nimiṣo 'nimiṣaḥ sragvī vācaspatir udāradhīḥ. (23)
slokas.text:
गुरुर्गुरुतमो धाम सत्यः सत्यपराक्रमः ।
निमिषोऽनिमिषः स्रग्वी वाचस्पतिरुदारधीः ॥२३॥

gurur gurutamo dhāma satyaḥ satyaparākramaḥ,
nimiṣo 'nimiṣaḥ sragvī vācaspatir udāradhīḥ. (23)

slokas.devanagari:
अग्रणीर्ग्रामणीः श्रीमान् न्यायो नेता समीरणः ।
सहस्रमूर्धा विश्वात्मा सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात् ॥२४॥
slokas.roman:
agraṇīr grāmaṇīḥ śrīmān nyāyo netā samīraṇaḥ,
sahasramūrdhā viśvātmā sahasrākṣaḥ sahasrapāt. (24)
slokas.text:
अग्रणीर्ग्रामणीः श्रीमान् न्यायो नेता समीरणः ।
सहस्रमूर्धा विश्वात्मा सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात् ॥२४॥

agraṇīr grāmaṇīḥ śrīmān nyāyo netā samīraṇaḥ,
sahasramūrdhā viśvātmā sahasrākṣaḥ sahasrapāt. (24)

slokas.devanagari:
आवर्तनो निवृत्तात्मा संवृतः सम्प्रमर्दनः।
अहः संवर्तको वह्निरनिलो धरणीधरः
॥२५॥
slokas.roman:
āvartano nivṛttātmā saṃvṛtaḥ saṃpramardanaḥ,
ahaḥ saṃvartako vahnir anilo dharaṇīdharaḥ. (25)
slokas.text:
आवर्तनो निवृत्तात्मा संवृतः सम्प्रमर्दनः।
अहः संवर्तको वह्निरनिलो धरणीधरः
॥२५॥

āvartano nivṛttātmā saṃvṛtaḥ saṃpramardanaḥ,
ahaḥ saṃvartako vahnir anilo dharaṇīdharaḥ. (25)

slokas.devanagari:
सुप्रसादः प्रसन्नात्मा विश्वधृग्विश्वभुग्विभुः ।
सत्कर्ता सत्कृतः साधुर्जह्नुर्नारायणो नरः ॥२६॥
slokas.roman:
suprasādaḥ prasannātmā viśvadhṛg viśvabhug vibhuḥ,
satkartā satkṛtaḥ sādhur jahnur nārāyaṇo naraḥ. (26)
slokas.text:
सुप्रसादः प्रसन्नात्मा विश्वधृग्विश्वभुग्विभुः ।
सत्कर्ता सत्कृतः साधुर्जह्नुर्नारायणो नरः ॥२६॥

suprasādaḥ prasannātmā viśvadhṛg viśvabhug vibhuḥ,
satkartā satkṛtaḥ sādhur jahnur nārāyaṇo naraḥ. (26)

slokas.devanagari:
असङ्ख्येयोऽप्रमेयात्मा विशिष्टः शिष्टकृच्छुचिः ।
सिद्धार्थः सिद्धसङ्कल्पः सिद्धिदः सिद्धिसाधनः ॥२७॥
slokas.roman:
asaṅkhyeyo 'prameyātmā viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭakṛc chuciḥ,
siddhārthaḥ siddhasaṅkalpaḥ siddhidaḥ siddhisādhanaḥ. (27)
slokas.text:
असङ्ख्येयोऽप्रमेयात्मा विशिष्टः शिष्टकृच्छुचिः ।
सिद्धार्थः सिद्धसङ्कल्पः सिद्धिदः सिद्धिसाधनः ॥२७॥

asaṅkhyeyo 'prameyātmā viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭakṛc chuciḥ,
siddhārthaḥ siddhasaṅkalpaḥ siddhidaḥ siddhisādhanaḥ. (27)

slokas.devanagari:
वृषाही वृषभो विष्णुर्वृषपर्वा वृषोदरः ।
वर्धनो वर्धमानश्च विविक्तः श्रुतिसागरः ॥२८॥
slokas.roman:
vṛṣāhī vṛṣabho viṣṇur vṛṣaparvā vṛṣodaraḥ,
vardhano vardhamānaś ca viviktaḥ śrutisāgaraḥ. (28)
slokas.text:
वृषाही वृषभो विष्णुर्वृषपर्वा वृषोदरः ।
वर्धनो वर्धमानश्च विविक्तः श्रुतिसागरः ॥२८॥

vṛṣāhī vṛṣabho viṣṇur vṛṣaparvā vṛṣodaraḥ,
vardhano vardhamānaś ca viviktaḥ śrutisāgaraḥ. (28)

slokas.devanagari:
सुभुजो दुर्धरो वाग्मी महेन्द्रो वसुदो वसुः ।
नैकरूपो बृहद्रूपः शिपिविष्टः प्रकाशनः ॥२९॥
slokas.roman:
subhujo durddharo vāgmī mahendro vasudo vasuḥ,
naikarūpo bṛhadrūpaḥ śipiviṣṭaḥ prakāśanaḥ. (29)
slokas.text:
सुभुजो दुर्धरो वाग्मी महेन्द्रो वसुदो वसुः ।
नैकरूपो बृहद्रूपः शिपिविष्टः प्रकाशनः ॥२९॥

subhujo durddharo vāgmī mahendro vasudo vasuḥ,
naikarūpo bṛhadrūpaḥ śipiviṣṭaḥ prakāśanaḥ. (29)

slokas.devanagari:
ओजस्तेजोद्युतिधरः प्रकाशात्मा प्रतापनः ।
ऋद्धः स्पष्टाक्षरो मन्त्रश्चन्द्रांशुर्भास्करद्युतिः ॥३०॥
slokas.roman:
ojas tejo dyutidharaḥ prakāśātmā pratāpanaḥ,
ṛddhaḥ spaṣṭākṣaro mantraś candrāṃśur bhāskaradyutiḥ. (30)
slokas.text:
ओजस्तेजोद्युतिधरः प्रकाशात्मा प्रतापनः ।
ऋद्धः स्पष्टाक्षरो मन्त्रश्चन्द्रांशुर्भास्करद्युतिः ॥३०॥

ojas tejo dyutidharaḥ prakāśātmā pratāpanaḥ,
ṛddhaḥ spaṣṭākṣaro mantraś candrāṃśur bhāskaradyutiḥ. (30)

slokas.devanagari:
अमृतांशूद्भवो भानुः शशबिन्दुः सुरेश्वरः ।
औषधं जगतः सेतुः सत्यधर्मपराक्रमः ॥३१॥
slokas.roman:
amṛtāṃśūdbhavo bhānuḥ śaśabinduḥ sureśvaraḥ,
auṣadhaṃ jagataḥ setuḥ satyadharmaparākramaḥ. (31)
slokas.text:
अमृतांशूद्भवो भानुः शशबिन्दुः सुरेश्वरः ।
औषधं जगतः सेतुः सत्यधर्मपराक्रमः ॥३१॥

amṛtāṃśūdbhavo bhānuḥ śaśabinduḥ sureśvaraḥ,
auṣadhaṃ jagataḥ setuḥ satyadharmaparākramaḥ. (31)

slokas.devanagari:
भूतभव्यभवन्नाथः पवनः पावनोऽनलः ।
कामहा कामकृत्कान्तः कामः कामप्रदः प्रभुः ॥३२॥
slokas.roman:
bhūtabhavyabhavannāthaḥ pavanaḥ pāvano 'nalaḥ,
kāmahā kāmakṛt kāntaḥ kāmaḥ kāmapradaḥ prabhuḥ. (32)
slokas.text:
भूतभव्यभवन्नाथः पवनः पावनोऽनलः ।
कामहा कामकृत्कान्तः कामः कामप्रदः प्रभुः ॥३२॥

bhūtabhavyabhavannāthaḥ pavanaḥ pāvano 'nalaḥ,
kāmahā kāmakṛt kāntaḥ kāmaḥ kāmapradaḥ prabhuḥ. (32)

slokas.devanagari:
युगादिकृद्युगावर्तो नैकमायो महाशनः ।
अदृश्यो व्यक्तरूपश्च सहस्रजिदनन्तजित् ॥३३॥
slokas.roman:
yugādikṛd yugāvarto naikamāyo mahāśanaḥ,
adṛśyo vyaktarūpaś ca sahasrajid anantajit. (33)
slokas.text:
युगादिकृद्युगावर्तो नैकमायो महाशनः ।
अदृश्यो व्यक्तरूपश्च सहस्रजिदनन्तजित् ॥३३॥

yugādikṛd yugāvarto naikamāyo mahāśanaḥ,
adṛśyo vyaktarūpaś ca sahasrajid anantajit. (33)

slokas.devanagari:
इष्टोऽविशिष्टः शिष्टेष्टः शिखण्डी नहुषो वृषः ।
क्रोधहा क्रोधकृत्कर्ता विश्वबाहुर्महीधरः ॥३४॥
slokas.roman:
iṣṭo 'viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭeṣṭaḥ śikhaṇḍī nahuṣo vṛṣaḥ,
krodhahā krodhakṛt kartā viśvabāhur mahīdharaḥ. (34)
slokas.text:
इष्टोऽविशिष्टः शिष्टेष्टः शिखण्डी नहुषो वृषः ।
क्रोधहा क्रोधकृत्कर्ता विश्वबाहुर्महीधरः ॥३४॥

iṣṭo 'viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭeṣṭaḥ śikhaṇḍī nahuṣo vṛṣaḥ,
krodhahā krodhakṛt kartā viśvabāhur mahīdharaḥ. (34)

slokas.devanagari:
अच्युतः प्रथितः प्राणः प्राणदो वासवानुजः ।
अपांनिधिरधिष्ठानमप्रमत्तः प्रतिष्ठितः ॥३५॥
slokas.roman:
acyutaḥ prathitaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇado vāsavānujaḥ,
apāṃ nidhir adhiṣṭhānam apramattaḥ pratiṣṭhitaḥ. (35)
slokas.text:
अच्युतः प्रथितः प्राणः प्राणदो वासवानुजः ।
अपांनिधिरधिष्ठानमप्रमत्तः प्रतिष्ठितः ॥३५॥

acyutaḥ prathitaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇado vāsavānujaḥ,
apāṃ nidhir adhiṣṭhānam apramattaḥ pratiṣṭhitaḥ. (35)

slokas.devanagari:
स्कन्दः स्कन्दधरो धुर्यो वरदो वायुवाहनः ।
वासुदेवो बृहद्भानुरादिदेवः पुरन्दरः ॥३६॥
slokas.roman:
skandaḥ skandadharo dhuryo varado vāyuvāhanaḥ,
vāsudevo bṛhadbhānur ādidevaḥ purandaraḥ. (36)
slokas.text:
स्कन्दः स्कन्दधरो धुर्यो वरदो वायुवाहनः ।
वासुदेवो बृहद्भानुरादिदेवः पुरन्दरः ॥३६॥

skandaḥ skandadharo dhuryo varado vāyuvāhanaḥ,
vāsudevo bṛhadbhānur ādidevaḥ purandaraḥ. (36)

slokas.devanagari:
अशोकस्तारणस्तारः शूरः शौरिर्जनेश्वरः ।
अनुकूलः शतावर्तः पद्मी पद्मनिभेक्षणः ॥३७॥
slokas.roman:
aśokas tāraṇas tāraḥ śūraḥ śaurir janeśvaraḥ,
anukūlaḥ śatāvartaḥ padmī padmanibhekṣaṇaḥ. (37)
slokas.text:
अशोकस्तारणस्तारः शूरः शौरिर्जनेश्वरः ।
अनुकूलः शतावर्तः पद्मी पद्मनिभेक्षणः ॥३७॥

aśokas tāraṇas tāraḥ śūraḥ śaurir janeśvaraḥ,
anukūlaḥ śatāvartaḥ padmī padmanibhekṣaṇaḥ. (37)

slokas.devanagari:
पद्मनाभोऽरविन्दाक्षः पद्मगर्भः शरीरभृत् ।
महर्द्धिरृद्धो वृद्धात्मा महाक्षो गरुडध्वजः ॥३८॥
slokas.roman:
padmanābho 'ravindākṣaḥ padmagarbhaḥ śarīrabhṛt,
maharddhir ṛddho vṛddhātmā mahākṣo garuḍadhvajaḥ. (38)
slokas.text:
पद्मनाभोऽरविन्दाक्षः पद्मगर्भः शरीरभृत् ।
महर्द्धिरृद्धो वृद्धात्मा महाक्षो गरुडध्वजः ॥३८॥

padmanābho 'ravindākṣaḥ padmagarbhaḥ śarīrabhṛt,
maharddhir ṛddho vṛddhātmā mahākṣo garuḍadhvajaḥ. (38)

slokas.devanagari:
अतुलः शरभो भीमः समयज्ञो हविर्हरिः ।
सर्वलक्षणलक्षण्यो लक्ष्मीवान् समितिञ्जयः ॥३९॥
slokas.roman:
atulaḥ śarabho bhīmaḥ samayajño havirhariḥ,
sarvalakṣaṇalakṣaṇyo lakṣmīvān samitiñjayaḥ. (39)
slokas.text:
अतुलः शरभो भीमः समयज्ञो हविर्हरिः ।
सर्वलक्षणलक्षण्यो लक्ष्मीवान् समितिञ्जयः ॥३९॥

atulaḥ śarabho bhīmaḥ samayajño havirhariḥ,
sarvalakṣaṇalakṣaṇyo lakṣmīvān samitiñjayaḥ. (39)

slokas.devanagari:
विक्षरो रोहितो मार्गो हेतुर्दामोदरः सहः ।
महीधरो महाभागो वेगवानमिताशनः ॥४०॥
slokas.roman:
vikṣaro rohito mārgo hetur dāmodaraḥ sahaḥ,
mahīdharo mahābhāgo vegavān amitāśanaḥ. (40)
slokas.text:
विक्षरो रोहितो मार्गो हेतुर्दामोदरः सहः ।
महीधरो महाभागो वेगवानमिताशनः ॥४०॥

vikṣaro rohito mārgo hetur dāmodaraḥ sahaḥ,
mahīdharo mahābhāgo vegavān amitāśanaḥ. (40)

slokas.devanagari:
उद्भवः क्षोभणो देवः श्रीगर्भः परमेश्वरः ।
करणं कारणं कर्ता विकर्ता गहनो गुहः ॥४१॥
slokas.roman:
udbhavaḥ kṣobhaṇo devaḥ śrīgarbhaḥ parameśvaraḥ,
karaṇaṃ kāraṇaṃ kartā vikartā gahano guhaḥ. (41)
slokas.text:
उद्भवः क्षोभणो देवः श्रीगर्भः परमेश्वरः ।
करणं कारणं कर्ता विकर्ता गहनो गुहः ॥४१॥

udbhavaḥ kṣobhaṇo devaḥ śrīgarbhaḥ parameśvaraḥ,
karaṇaṃ kāraṇaṃ kartā vikartā gahano guhaḥ. (41)

slokas.devanagari:
व्यवसायो व्यवस्थानः संस्थानः स्थानदो ध्रुवः ।
परर्द्धिः परमस्पष्टस्तुष्टः पुष्टः शुभेक्षणः ॥४२॥
slokas.roman:
vyavasāyo vyavasthānaḥ saṃsthānaḥ sthānado dhruvaḥ,
pararddhiḥ paramaspaṣṭas tuṣṭaḥ puṣṭaḥ śubhekṣaṇaḥ. (42)
slokas.text:
व्यवसायो व्यवस्थानः संस्थानः स्थानदो ध्रुवः ।
परर्द्धिः परमस्पष्टस्तुष्टः पुष्टः शुभेक्षणः ॥४२॥

vyavasāyo vyavasthānaḥ saṃsthānaḥ sthānado dhruvaḥ,
pararddhiḥ paramaspaṣṭas tuṣṭaḥ puṣṭaḥ śubhekṣaṇaḥ. (42)

slokas.devanagari:
रामो विरामो विरजो मार्गो नेयो नयोऽनयः ।
वीरः शक्तिमतां श्रेष्ठो धर्मो धर्मविदुत्तमः ॥४३॥
slokas.roman:
rāmo virāmo virajo mārgo neyo nayo 'nayaḥ,
vīraḥ śaktimatāṃ śreṣṭho dharmo dharmaviduttamaḥ. (43)
slokas.text:
रामो विरामो विरजो मार्गो नेयो नयोऽनयः ।
वीरः शक्तिमतां श्रेष्ठो धर्मो धर्मविदुत्तमः ॥४३॥

rāmo virāmo virajo mārgo neyo nayo 'nayaḥ,
vīraḥ śaktimatāṃ śreṣṭho dharmo dharmaviduttamaḥ. (43)

slokas.devanagari:
वैकुण्ठः पुरुषः प्राणः प्राणदः प्रणवः पृथुः ।
हिरण्यगर्भः शत्रुघ्नो व्याप्तो वायुरधोक्षजः ॥४४॥
slokas.roman:
vaikuṇṭhaḥ puruṣaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇadaḥ praṇavaḥ pṛthuḥ,
hiraṇyagarbhaḥ śatrughno vyāpto vāyur adhokṣajaḥ. (44)
slokas.text:
वैकुण्ठः पुरुषः प्राणः प्राणदः प्रणवः पृथुः ।
हिरण्यगर्भः शत्रुघ्नो व्याप्तो वायुरधोक्षजः ॥४४॥

vaikuṇṭhaḥ puruṣaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇadaḥ praṇavaḥ pṛthuḥ,
hiraṇyagarbhaḥ śatrughno vyāpto vāyur adhokṣajaḥ. (44)

slokas.devanagari:
ऋतुः सुदर्शनः कालः परमेष्ठी परिग्रहः ।
उग्रः संवत्सरो दक्षो विश्रामो विश्वदक्षिणः ॥४५॥
slokas.roman:
ṛtuḥ sudarśanaḥ kālaḥ parameṣṭhī parigrahaḥ,
ugraḥ saṃvatsaro dakṣo viśrāmo viśvadakṣiṇaḥ. (45)
slokas.text:
ऋतुः सुदर्शनः कालः परमेष्ठी परिग्रहः ।
उग्रः संवत्सरो दक्षो विश्रामो विश्वदक्षिणः ॥४५॥

ṛtuḥ sudarśanaḥ kālaḥ parameṣṭhī parigrahaḥ,
ugraḥ saṃvatsaro dakṣo viśrāmo viśvadakṣiṇaḥ. (45)

slokas.devanagari:
विस्तारः स्थावरस्थाणुः प्रमाणं बीजमव्ययम् ।
अर्थोऽनर्थो महाकोशो महाभोगो महाधनः ॥४६॥
slokas.roman:
vistāraḥ sthāvaraḥ sthāṇuḥ pramāṇaṃ bījam avyayam,
artho 'nartho mahākośo mahābhogo mahādhanaḥ. (46)
slokas.text:
विस्तारः स्थावरस्थाणुः प्रमाणं बीजमव्ययम् ।
अर्थोऽनर्थो महाकोशो महाभोगो महाधनः ॥४६॥

vistāraḥ sthāvaraḥ sthāṇuḥ pramāṇaṃ bījam avyayam,
artho 'nartho mahākośo mahābhogo mahādhanaḥ. (46)

slokas.devanagari:
अनिर्विण्णः स्थविष्ठोऽभूर्धर्मयूपो महामखः ।
नक्षत्रनेमिर्नक्षत्री क्षमः क्षामः समीहनः ॥४७॥
slokas.roman:
anirviṇṇaḥ sthaviṣṭho 'bhūr dharmayūpo mahāmakhaḥ,
nakṣatranemiḥ nakṣatrī kṣamaḥ kṣāmaḥ samīhanaḥ. (47)
slokas.text:
अनिर्विण्णः स्थविष्ठोऽभूर्धर्मयूपो महामखः ।
नक्षत्रनेमिर्नक्षत्री क्षमः क्षामः समीहनः ॥४७॥

anirviṇṇaḥ sthaviṣṭho 'bhūr dharmayūpo mahāmakhaḥ,
nakṣatranemiḥ nakṣatrī kṣamaḥ kṣāmaḥ samīhanaḥ. (47)

slokas.devanagari:
यज्ञ इज्यो महेज्यश्च क्रतुः सत्रं सतां गतिः ।
सर्वदर्शी विमुक्तात्मा सर्वज्ञो ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ॥४८॥
slokas.roman:
yajña ijyo mahejyaś ca kratuḥ satraṃ satāṃ gatiḥ,
sarvadarśī vimuktātmā sarvajño jñānam uttamam. (48)
slokas.text:
यज्ञ इज्यो महेज्यश्च क्रतुः सत्रं सतां गतिः ।
सर्वदर्शी विमुक्तात्मा सर्वज्ञो ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ॥४८॥

yajña ijyo mahejyaś ca kratuḥ satraṃ satāṃ gatiḥ,
sarvadarśī vimuktātmā sarvajño jñānam uttamam. (48)

slokas.devanagari:
सुव्रतः सुमुखः सूक्ष्मः सुघोषः सुखदः सुहृत् ।
मनोहरो जितक्रोधो वीरबाहुर्विदारणः ॥४९॥
slokas.roman:
suvrataḥ sumukhaḥ sūkṣmaḥ sughoṣaḥ sukhadaḥ suhṛt,
manoharaḥ jitakrodho vīrabāhur vidāraṇaḥ. (49)
slokas.text:
सुव्रतः सुमुखः सूक्ष्मः सुघोषः सुखदः सुहृत् ।
मनोहरो जितक्रोधो वीरबाहुर्विदारणः ॥४९॥

suvrataḥ sumukhaḥ sūkṣmaḥ sughoṣaḥ sukhadaḥ suhṛt,
manoharaḥ jitakrodho vīrabāhur vidāraṇaḥ. (49)

slokas.devanagari:
स्वापनः स्ववशो व्यापी नैकात्मा नैककर्मकृत् ।
वत्सरो वत्सलो वत्सी रत्नगर्भो धनेश्वरः ॥५०॥
slokas.roman:
svāpanaḥ svavaśo vyāpī naikātmā naikakarmakṛt,
vatsaro vatsalo vatsī ratnagarbho dDDhaneśvaraḥ. (50)
slokas.text:
स्वापनः स्ववशो व्यापी नैकात्मा नैककर्मकृत् ।
वत्सरो वत्सलो वत्सी रत्नगर्भो धनेश्वरः ॥५०॥

svāpanaḥ svavaśo vyāpī naikātmā naikakarmakṛt,
vatsaro vatsalo vatsī ratnagarbho dDDhaneśvaraḥ. (50)

slokas.devanagari:
धर्मगुब्धर्मकृद्धर्मी सदसत्क्षरमक्षरम् ।
अविज्ञाता सहस्रांशुर्विधाता कृतलक्षणः ॥५१॥
slokas.roman:
dharmagub dharmakṛd dharmī sadasat kṣaram akṣaram,
avijñātā sahasrāṃśur vidhātā kṛtalakṣaṇaḥ. (51)
slokas.text:
धर्मगुब्धर्मकृद्धर्मी सदसत्क्षरमक्षरम् ।
अविज्ञाता सहस्रांशुर्विधाता कृतलक्षणः ॥५१॥

dharmagub dharmakṛd dharmī sadasat kṣaram akṣaram,
avijñātā sahasrāṃśur vidhātā kṛtalakṣaṇaḥ. (51)

slokas.devanagari:
गभस्तिनेमिः सत्त्वस्थः सिंहो भूतमहेश्वरः ।
आदिदेवो महादेवो देवेशो देवभृद्गुरुः ॥५२॥
slokas.roman:
gabhastinemiḥ sattvasthaḥ siṃho bhūtamaheśvaraḥ,
ādidevo mahādevo deveśo devabhṛd guruḥ. (52)
slokas.text:
गभस्तिनेमिः सत्त्वस्थः सिंहो भूतमहेश्वरः ।
आदिदेवो महादेवो देवेशो देवभृद्गुरुः ॥५२॥

gabhastinemiḥ sattvasthaḥ siṃho bhūtamaheśvaraḥ,
ādidevo mahādevo deveśo devabhṛd guruḥ. (52)

slokas.devanagari:
उत्तरो गोपतिर्गोप्ता ज्ञानगम्यः पुरातनः ।
शरीरभूतभृद्भोक्ता कपीन्द्रो भूरिदक्षिणः ॥५३॥
slokas.roman:
uttaro gopatiḥ goptā jñānagamyaḥ purātanaḥ,
śarīrabhūtabhṛd bhoktā kapīndro bhūridakṣiṇaḥ. (53)
slokas.text:
उत्तरो गोपतिर्गोप्ता ज्ञानगम्यः पुरातनः ।
शरीरभूतभृद्भोक्ता कपीन्द्रो भूरिदक्षिणः ॥५३॥

uttaro gopatiḥ goptā jñānagamyaḥ purātanaḥ,
śarīrabhūtabhṛd bhoktā kapīndro bhūridakṣiṇaḥ. (53)

slokas.devanagari:
सोमपोऽमृतपः सोमः पुरुजित्पुरुसत्तमः ।
विनयो जयः सत्यसन्धो दाशार्हः सात्वताम्पतिः ॥५४॥
slokas.roman:
somapo 'mṛtapaḥ somaḥ purujit purusattamaḥ,
vinayo jayaḥ satyasandho dāśārhaḥ sātvatāṃ patiḥ. (54)
slokas.text:
सोमपोऽमृतपः सोमः पुरुजित्पुरुसत्तमः ।
विनयो जयः सत्यसन्धो दाशार्हः सात्वताम्पतिः ॥५४॥

somapo 'mṛtapaḥ somaḥ purujit purusattamaḥ,
vinayo jayaḥ satyasandho dāśārhaḥ sātvatāṃ patiḥ. (54)

slokas.devanagari:
जीवो विनयिता साक्षी मुकुन्दोऽमितविक्रमः ।
अम्भोनिधिरनन्तात्मा महोदधिशयोऽन्तकः ॥५५॥
slokas.roman:
jīvo vinayitā sākṣī mukundo 'mitavikramaḥ,
ambhonidhir anantātmā mahodadhiśayo 'ntakaḥ. (55)
slokas.text:
जीवो विनयिता साक्षी मुकुन्दोऽमितविक्रमः ।
अम्भोनिधिरनन्तात्मा महोदधिशयोऽन्तकः ॥५५॥

jīvo vinayitā sākṣī mukundo 'mitavikramaḥ,
ambhonidhir anantātmā mahodadhiśayo 'ntakaḥ. (55)

slokas.devanagari:
अजो महार्हः स्वाभाव्यो जितामित्रः प्रमोदनः ।
आनन्दो नन्दनो नन्दः सत्यधर्मा त्रिविक्रमः ॥५६॥
slokas.roman:
ajo mahārhaḥ svābhāvyo jitāmitraḥ pramodanaḥ,
ānando nandano nandaḥ satyadharmā trivikramaḥ. (56)
slokas.text:
अजो महार्हः स्वाभाव्यो जितामित्रः प्रमोदनः ।
आनन्दो नन्दनो नन्दः सत्यधर्मा त्रिविक्रमः ॥५६॥

ajo mahārhaḥ svābhāvyo jitāmitraḥ pramodanaḥ,
ānando nandano nandaḥ satyadharmā trivikramaḥ. (56)

slokas.devanagari:
महर्षिः कपिलाचार्यः कृतज्ञो मेदिनीपतिः ।
त्रिपदस्त्रिदशाध्यक्षो महाशङ्गः कृतान्तकृत् ॥५७॥
slokas.roman:
maharṣiḥ kapilācāryaḥ kṛtajño medinīpatiḥ,
tripadas tridaśādhyakṣo mahāśṛṅgaḥ kṛtāntakṛt. (57)
slokas.text:
महर्षिः कपिलाचार्यः कृतज्ञो मेदिनीपतिः ।
त्रिपदस्त्रिदशाध्यक्षो महाशङ्गः कृतान्तकृत् ॥५७॥

maharṣiḥ kapilācāryaḥ kṛtajño medinīpatiḥ,
tripadas tridaśādhyakṣo mahāśṛṅgaḥ kṛtāntakṛt. (57)

slokas.devanagari:
महावराहो गोविन्दः सुषेणः कनकाङ्गदी ।
गुह्यो गभीरो गहनो गुप्तश्चक्रगदाधरः ॥५८॥
slokas.roman:
mahāvarāho govindaḥ suṣeṇaḥ kanakāṅgadī,
guhyo gabhīro gahano guptaś cakragadādharaḥ. (58)
slokas.text:
महावराहो गोविन्दः सुषेणः कनकाङ्गदी ।
गुह्यो गभीरो गहनो गुप्तश्चक्रगदाधरः ॥५८॥

mahāvarāho govindaḥ suṣeṇaḥ kanakāṅgadī,
guhyo gabhīro gahano guptaś cakragadādharaḥ. (58)

slokas.devanagari:
वेधाः स्वाङ्गोऽजितः कृष्णो दृढः सङ्कर्षणोऽच्युतः ।
वरुणो वारुणो वृक्षः पुष्कराक्षो महामनाः ॥५९॥
slokas.roman:
vedhāḥ svāṅgo 'jitaḥ kṛṣṇo dṛḍhaḥ saṅkarṣaṇo 'cyutaḥ,
varuṇo vāruṇo vṛkṣaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahāmanāḥ. (59)
slokas.text:
वेधाः स्वाङ्गोऽजितः कृष्णो दृढः सङ्कर्षणोऽच्युतः ।
वरुणो वारुणो वृक्षः पुष्कराक्षो महामनाः ॥५९॥

vedhāḥ svāṅgo 'jitaḥ kṛṣṇo dṛḍhaḥ saṅkarṣaṇo 'cyutaḥ,
varuṇo vāruṇo vṛkṣaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahāmanāḥ. (59)

slokas.devanagari:
भगवान् भगहाऽऽनन्दी वनमाली हलायुधः ।
आदित्यो ज्योतिरादित्यः सहिष्णुर्गतिसत्तमः ॥६०॥
slokas.roman:
bhagavān bhagahānandī vanamālī halāyudhaḥ,
ādityo jyotir ādityaḥ sahiṣṇur gatisattamaḥ. (60)
slokas.text:
भगवान् भगहाऽऽनन्दी वनमाली हलायुधः ।
आदित्यो ज्योतिरादित्यः सहिष्णुर्गतिसत्तमः ॥६०॥

bhagavān bhagahānandī vanamālī halāyudhaḥ,
ādityo jyotir ādityaḥ sahiṣṇur gatisattamaḥ. (60)

slokas.devanagari:
सुधन्वा खण्डपरशुर्दारुणो द्रविणप्रदः ।
दिवस्पृक् सर्वदृग्व्यासो वाचस्पतिरयोनिजः ॥६१॥
slokas.roman:
sudhanvā khaṇḍaparaśur dāruṇo draviṇapradaḥ,
divaḥspṛk sarvadṛg vyāso vācaspatir ayonijaḥ. (61)
slokas.text:
सुधन्वा खण्डपरशुर्दारुणो द्रविणप्रदः ।
दिवस्पृक् सर्वदृग्व्यासो वाचस्पतिरयोनिजः ॥६१॥

sudhanvā khaṇḍaparaśur dāruṇo draviṇapradaḥ,
divaḥspṛk sarvadṛg vyāso vācaspatir ayonijaḥ. (61)

slokas.devanagari:
त्रिसामा सामगः साम निर्वाणं भेषजं भिषक् ।
संन्यासकृच्छमः शान्तो निष्ठा शान्तिः परायणम् ॥६२॥
slokas.roman:
trisāmā sāmagaḥ sāma nirvāṇaṃ bheṣajaṃ bhiṣak,
saṃnyāsakṛc chamaḥ śānto niṣṭhā śāntiḥ parāyaṇam. (62)
slokas.text:
त्रिसामा सामगः साम निर्वाणं भेषजं भिषक् ।
संन्यासकृच्छमः शान्तो निष्ठा शान्तिः परायणम् ॥६२॥

trisāmā sāmagaḥ sāma nirvāṇaṃ bheṣajaṃ bhiṣak,
saṃnyāsakṛc chamaḥ śānto niṣṭhā śāntiḥ parāyaṇam. (62)

slokas.devanagari:
शुभाङ्गः शान्तिदः स्रष्टा कुमुदः कुवलेशयः ।
गोहितो गोपतिर्गोप्ता वृषभाक्षो वृषप्रियः ॥६३॥
slokas.roman:
śubhāṅgaḥ śāntidaḥ sraṣṭā kumudaḥ kuvaleśayaḥ,
gohito gopatiḥ goptā vṛṣabhākṣo vṛṣapriyaḥ. (63)
slokas.text:
शुभाङ्गः शान्तिदः स्रष्टा कुमुदः कुवलेशयः ।
गोहितो गोपतिर्गोप्ता वृषभाक्षो वृषप्रियः ॥६३॥

śubhāṅgaḥ śāntidaḥ sraṣṭā kumudaḥ kuvaleśayaḥ,
gohito gopatiḥ goptā vṛṣabhākṣo vṛṣapriyaḥ. (63)

slokas.devanagari:
अनिवर्ती निवृत्तात्मा सङ्क्षेप्ता क्षेमकृच्छिवः ।
श्रीवत्सवक्षाः श्रीवासः श्रीपतिः श्रीमतांवरः ॥६४॥
slokas.roman:
anivartī nivṛttātmā saṃkṣeptā kṣemakṛc chivaḥ,
śrīvatsavakṣāḥ śrīvāsaḥ śrīpatiḥ śrīmatāṃ varaḥ. (64)
slokas.text:
अनिवर्ती निवृत्तात्मा सङ्क्षेप्ता क्षेमकृच्छिवः ।
श्रीवत्सवक्षाः श्रीवासः श्रीपतिः श्रीमतांवरः ॥६४॥

anivartī nivṛttātmā saṃkṣeptā kṣemakṛc chivaḥ,
śrīvatsavakṣāḥ śrīvāsaḥ śrīpatiḥ śrīmatāṃ varaḥ. (64)

slokas.devanagari:
श्रीदः श्रीशः श्रीनिवासः श्रीनिधिः श्रीविभावनः ।
श्रीधरः श्रीकरः श्रेयः श्रीमाँल्लोकत्रयाश्रयः ॥६५॥
slokas.roman:
śrīdaḥ śrīśaḥ śrīnivāsaḥ śrīnidhiḥ śrīvibhāvanaḥ,
śrīdharaḥ śrīkaraḥ śreyaḥ śrīmān lokatrayāśrayaḥ. (65)
slokas.text:
श्रीदः श्रीशः श्रीनिवासः श्रीनिधिः श्रीविभावनः ।
श्रीधरः श्रीकरः श्रेयः श्रीमाँल्लोकत्रयाश्रयः ॥६५॥

śrīdaḥ śrīśaḥ śrīnivāsaḥ śrīnidhiḥ śrīvibhāvanaḥ,
śrīdharaḥ śrīkaraḥ śreyaḥ śrīmān lokatrayāśrayaḥ. (65)

slokas.devanagari:
स्वक्षः स्वङ्गः शतानन्दो नन्दिर्ज्योतिर्गणेश्वरः ।
विजितात्माऽविधेयात्मा सत्कीर्तिश्छिन्नसंशयः ॥६६॥
slokas.roman:
svakṣaḥ svaṅgaḥ śatānando nandir jyotirgaṇeśvaraḥ,
vijitātmā vidheyātmā satkīrtiś chinnasaṃśayaḥ. (66)
slokas.text:
स्वक्षः स्वङ्गः शतानन्दो नन्दिर्ज्योतिर्गणेश्वरः ।
विजितात्माऽविधेयात्मा सत्कीर्तिश्छिन्नसंशयः ॥६६॥

svakṣaḥ svaṅgaḥ śatānando nandir jyotirgaṇeśvaraḥ,
vijitātmā vidheyātmā satkīrtiś chinnasaṃśayaḥ. (66)

slokas.devanagari:
उदीर्णः सर्वतश्चक्षुरनीशः शाश्वतस्थिरः ।
भूशयो भूषणो भूतिर्विशोकः शोकनाशनः ॥६७॥
slokas.roman:
udīrṇaḥ sarvataś cakṣur anīśaḥ śāśvatasthiraḥ,
bhūśayo bhūṣaṇo bhūtir viśokaḥ śokanāśanaḥ. (67)
slokas.text:
उदीर्णः सर्वतश्चक्षुरनीशः शाश्वतस्थिरः ।
भूशयो भूषणो भूतिर्विशोकः शोकनाशनः ॥६७॥

udīrṇaḥ sarvataś cakṣur anīśaḥ śāśvatasthiraḥ,
bhūśayo bhūṣaṇo bhūtir viśokaḥ śokanāśanaḥ. (67)

slokas.devanagari:
अर्चिष्मानर्चितः कुम्भो विशुद्धात्मा विशोधनः ।
अनिरुद्धोऽप्रतिरथः प्रद्युम्नोऽमितविक्रमः ॥६८॥
slokas.roman:
arciṣmān arcitaḥ kumbho viśuddhātmā viśodhanaḥ,
aniruddho 'pratirathaḥ pradyumno 'mitavikramaḥ. (68)
slokas.text:
अर्चिष्मानर्चितः कुम्भो विशुद्धात्मा विशोधनः ।
अनिरुद्धोऽप्रतिरथः प्रद्युम्नोऽमितविक्रमः ॥६८॥

arciṣmān arcitaḥ kumbho viśuddhātmā viśodhanaḥ,
aniruddho 'pratirathaḥ pradyumno 'mitavikramaḥ. (68)

slokas.devanagari:
कालनेमिनिहा वीरः शौरिः शूरजनेश्वरः ।
त्रिलोकात्मा त्रिलोकेशः केशवः केशिहा हरिः ॥६९॥
slokas.roman:
kālaneminihā vīraḥ śauriḥ śūrajaneśvaraḥ,
trilokātmā trilokeśaḥ keśavaḥ keśihā hariḥ. (69)
slokas.text:
कालनेमिनिहा वीरः शौरिः शूरजनेश्वरः ।
त्रिलोकात्मा त्रिलोकेशः केशवः केशिहा हरिः ॥६९॥

kālaneminihā vīraḥ śauriḥ śūrajaneśvaraḥ,
trilokātmā trilokeśaḥ keśavaḥ keśihā hariḥ. (69)

slokas.devanagari:
कामदेवः कामपालः कामी कान्तः कृतागमः ।
अनिर्देश्यवपुर्विष्णुर्वीरोऽनन्तो धनञ्जयः ॥७०॥
slokas.roman:
kāmadevaḥ kāmapālaḥ kāmī kāntaḥ kṛtāgamaḥ,
anirdeśyavapuḥ viṣṇuḥ vīro 'nanto dhanañjayaḥ. (70)
slokas.text:
कामदेवः कामपालः कामी कान्तः कृतागमः ।
अनिर्देश्यवपुर्विष्णुर्वीरोऽनन्तो धनञ्जयः ॥७०॥

kāmadevaḥ kāmapālaḥ kāmī kāntaḥ kṛtāgamaḥ,
anirdeśyavapuḥ viṣṇuḥ vīro 'nanto dhanañjayaḥ. (70)

slokas.devanagari:
ब्रह्मण्यो ब्रह्मकृद् ब्रह्मा ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविवर्धनः ।
ब्रह्मविद् ब्राह्मणो ब्रह्मी ब्रह्मज्ञो ब्राह्मणप्रियः ॥७१॥
slokas.roman:
brahmaṇyo brahmakṛd brahmā brahma brahmavivardhanaḥ,
brahmavid brāhmaṇo brahmī brahmajño brāhmaṇapriyaḥ. (71)
slokas.text:
ब्रह्मण्यो ब्रह्मकृद् ब्रह्मा ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविवर्धनः ।
ब्रह्मविद् ब्राह्मणो ब्रह्मी ब्रह्मज्ञो ब्राह्मणप्रियः ॥७१॥

brahmaṇyo brahmakṛd brahmā brahma brahmavivardhanaḥ,
brahmavid brāhmaṇo brahmī brahmajño brāhmaṇapriyaḥ. (71)

slokas.devanagari:
महाक्रमो महाकर्मा महातेजा महोरगः ।
महाक्रतुर्महायज्वा महायज्ञो महाहविः ॥७२॥
slokas.roman:
mahākramo mahākarmā mahātejā mahoragaḥ,
mahākratur mahāyajvā mahāyajño mahāhaviḥ. (72)
slokas.text:
महाक्रमो महाकर्मा महातेजा महोरगः ।
महाक्रतुर्महायज्वा महायज्ञो महाहविः ॥७२॥

mahākramo mahākarmā mahātejā mahoragaḥ,
mahākratur mahāyajvā mahāyajño mahāhaviḥ. (72)

slokas.devanagari:
स्तव्यः स्तवप्रियः स्तोत्रं स्तुतिः स्तोता रणप्रियः ।
पूर्णः पूरयिता पुण्यः पुण्यकीर्तिरनामयः ॥७३॥
slokas.roman:
stavyaḥ stavapriyaḥ stotraṃ stutiḥ stotā raṇapriyaḥ,
pūrṇaḥ pūrayitā puṇyaḥ puṇyakīrtir anāmayaḥ. (73)
slokas.text:
स्तव्यः स्तवप्रियः स्तोत्रं स्तुतिः स्तोता रणप्रियः ।
पूर्णः पूरयिता पुण्यः पुण्यकीर्तिरनामयः ॥७३॥

stavyaḥ stavapriyaḥ stotraṃ stutiḥ stotā raṇapriyaḥ,
pūrṇaḥ pūrayitā puṇyaḥ puṇyakīrtir anāmayaḥ. (73)

slokas.devanagari:
मनोजवस्तीर्थकरो वसुरेता वसुप्रदः ।
वसुप्रदो वासुदेवो वसुर्वसुमना हविः ॥७४॥
slokas.roman:
manojavastīrthakaraḥ vasuretas vasupradaḥ,
vasuprado vāsudevo vasur vasumānā haviḥ. (74)
slokas.text:
मनोजवस्तीर्थकरो वसुरेता वसुप्रदः ।
वसुप्रदो वासुदेवो वसुर्वसुमना हविः ॥७४॥

manojavastīrthakaraḥ vasuretas vasupradaḥ,
vasuprado vāsudevo vasur vasumānā haviḥ. (74)

slokas.devanagari:
सद्गतिः सत्कृतिः सत्ता सद्भूतिः सत्परायणः ।
शूरसेनो यदुश्रेष्ठः सन्निवासः सुयामुनः ॥७५॥
slokas.roman:
sadgatiḥ satkṛtiḥ sattā sadbhūtiḥ satparāyaṇaḥ,
śūraseno yaduśreṣṭhaḥ sannivāsaḥ suyāmunaḥ. (75)
slokas.text:
सद्गतिः सत्कृतिः सत्ता सद्भूतिः सत्परायणः ।
शूरसेनो यदुश्रेष्ठः सन्निवासः सुयामुनः ॥७५॥

sadgatiḥ satkṛtiḥ sattā sadbhūtiḥ satparāyaṇaḥ,
śūraseno yaduśreṣṭhaḥ sannivāsaḥ suyāmunaḥ. (75)

slokas.devanagari:
भूतावासो वासुदेवः सर्वासुनिलयोऽनलः ।
दर्पहा दर्पदो दृप्तो दुर्धरोऽथापराजितः ॥७६॥
slokas.roman:
bhūtāvāso vāsudevaḥ sarvāsunilayo 'nalaḥ,
darpahā darpado dṛpto durddharo 'thāparājitaḥ. (76)
slokas.text:
भूतावासो वासुदेवः सर्वासुनिलयोऽनलः ।
दर्पहा दर्पदो दृप्तो दुर्धरोऽथापराजितः ॥७६॥

bhūtāvāso vāsudevaḥ sarvāsunilayo 'nalaḥ,
darpahā darpado dṛpto durddharo 'thāparājitaḥ. (76)

slokas.devanagari:
विश्वमूर्तिर्महामूर्तिर्दीप्तमूर्तिरमूर्तिमान् ।
अनेकमूर्तिरव्यक्तः शतमूर्तिः शताननः ॥७७॥
slokas.roman:
viśvamūrtir mahāmūrtir dīptamūrtir amūrtimān,
anekamūrtir avyaktaḥ śatamūrtiḥ śatānanaḥ. (77)
slokas.text:
विश्वमूर्तिर्महामूर्तिर्दीप्तमूर्तिरमूर्तिमान् ।
अनेकमूर्तिरव्यक्तः शतमूर्तिः शताननः ॥७७॥

viśvamūrtir mahāmūrtir dīptamūrtir amūrtimān,
anekamūrtir avyaktaḥ śatamūrtiḥ śatānanaḥ. (77)

slokas.devanagari:
एको नैकः सवः कः किं यत् तत्पदमनुत्तमम् ।
लोकबन्धुर्लोकनाथो माधवो भक्तवत्सलः ॥७८॥
slokas.roman:
eko naikaḥ savaḥ kaḥ kiṃ yat tat padam anuttamam,
lokabandhuḥ lokānātho mādhavo bhaktavatsalaḥ. (78)
slokas.text:
एको नैकः सवः कः किं यत् तत्पदमनुत्तमम् ।
लोकबन्धुर्लोकनाथो माधवो भक्तवत्सलः ॥७८॥

eko naikaḥ savaḥ kaḥ kiṃ yat tat padam anuttamam,
lokabandhuḥ lokānātho mādhavo bhaktavatsalaḥ. (78)

slokas.devanagari:
सुवर्णवर्णो हेमाङ्गो वराङ्गश्चन्दनाङ्गदी ।
वीरहा विषमः शून्यो घृताशीरचलश्चलः ॥७९॥
slokas.roman:
suvarṇavarṇo hemāṅgo varāṅgaś candanāṅgadī,
vīrahā viṣamaḥ śūnyo ghṛtāśīr acalaś calaḥ. (79)
slokas.text:
सुवर्णवर्णो हेमाङ्गो वराङ्गश्चन्दनाङ्गदी ।
वीरहा विषमः शून्यो घृताशीरचलश्चलः ॥७९॥

suvarṇavarṇo hemāṅgo varāṅgaś candanāṅgadī,
vīrahā viṣamaḥ śūnyo ghṛtāśīr acalaś calaḥ. (79)

slokas.devanagari:
अमानी मानदो मान्यो लोकस्वामी त्रिलोकधृक् ।
सुमेधा मेधजो धन्यः सत्यमेधा धराधरः ॥८०॥
slokas.roman:
amānī mānado mānyo lokasvāmī trilokadhṛk,
sumedhā medhajo dhanyaḥ satyamedhā dharādharaḥ. (80)
slokas.text:
अमानी मानदो मान्यो लोकस्वामी त्रिलोकधृक् ।
सुमेधा मेधजो धन्यः सत्यमेधा धराधरः ॥८०॥

amānī mānado mānyo lokasvāmī trilokadhṛk,
sumedhā medhajo dhanyaḥ satyamedhā dharādharaḥ. (80)

slokas.devanagari:
तेजोवृषो द्युतिधरः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।
प्रग्रहो निग्रहो व्यग्रो नैकशृङ्गो गदाग्रजः ॥८१॥
slokas.roman:
tejovṛṣo dyutidharaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṃ varaḥ,
pragraho nigraho vyagro naikaśṛṅgo gadāgrajaḥ. (81)
slokas.text:
तेजोवृषो द्युतिधरः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।
प्रग्रहो निग्रहो व्यग्रो नैकशृङ्गो गदाग्रजः ॥८१॥

tejovṛṣo dyutidharaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṃ varaḥ,
pragraho nigraho vyagro naikaśṛṅgo gadāgrajaḥ. (81)

slokas.devanagari:
चतुर्मूर्तिश्चतुर्बाहुश्चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्गतिः ।
चतुरात्मा चतुर्भावश्चतुर्वेदविदेकपात् ॥८२॥
slokas.roman:
caturmūrtiś caturbāhuś caturvyūhaś caturgatiḥ,
caturātmā caturbhāvaś caturvedavid ekapāt. (82)
slokas.text:
चतुर्मूर्तिश्चतुर्बाहुश्चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्गतिः ।
चतुरात्मा चतुर्भावश्चतुर्वेदविदेकपात् ॥८२॥

caturmūrtiś caturbāhuś caturvyūhaś caturgatiḥ,
caturātmā caturbhāvaś caturvedavid ekapāt. (82)

slokas.devanagari:
समावर्तोऽनिवृत्तात्मा दुर्जयो दुरतिक्रमः ।
दुर्लभो दुर्गमो दुर्गो दुरावासो दुरारिहा ॥८३॥
slokas.roman:
samāvarto 'nivṛttātmā durjayo duratikramaḥ,
durlabho durgamo durgo durāvāso durārihā. (83)
slokas.text:
समावर्तोऽनिवृत्तात्मा दुर्जयो दुरतिक्रमः ।
दुर्लभो दुर्गमो दुर्गो दुरावासो दुरारिहा ॥८३॥

samāvarto 'nivṛttātmā durjayo duratikramaḥ,
durlabho durgamo durgo durāvāso durārihā. (83)

slokas.devanagari:
शुभाङ्गो लोकसारङ्गः सुतन्तुस्तन्तुवर्धनः ।
इन्द्रकर्मा महाकर्मा कृतकर्मा कृतागमः ॥८४॥
slokas.roman:
śubhāṅgo lokasāraṅgaḥ sutantus tantuvardhanaḥ,
indrakarmā mahākarmā kṛtakarmā kṛtāgamaḥ. (84)
slokas.text:
शुभाङ्गो लोकसारङ्गः सुतन्तुस्तन्तुवर्धनः ।
इन्द्रकर्मा महाकर्मा कृतकर्मा कृतागमः ॥८४॥

śubhāṅgo lokasāraṅgaḥ sutantus tantuvardhanaḥ,
indrakarmā mahākarmā kṛtakarmā kṛtāgamaḥ. (84)

slokas.devanagari:
उद्भवः सुन्दरः सुन्दो रत्ननाभः सुलोचनः ।
अर्को वाजसनः शृङ्गी जयन्तः सर्वविज्जयी ॥८५॥
slokas.roman:
udbhavaḥ sundaraḥ sundo ratnanābhaḥ sulocanaḥ,
arko vājasanaḥ śṛṅgī jayantaḥ sarvavij jayī. (85)
slokas.text:
उद्भवः सुन्दरः सुन्दो रत्ननाभः सुलोचनः ।
अर्को वाजसनः शृङ्गी जयन्तः सर्वविज्जयी ॥८५॥

udbhavaḥ sundaraḥ sundo ratnanābhaḥ sulocanaḥ,
arko vājasanaḥ śṛṅgī jayantaḥ sarvavij jayī. (85)

slokas.devanagari:
सुवर्णबिन्दुरक्षोभ्यः सर्ववागीश्वरेश्वरः ।
महाह्रदो महागर्तो महाभूतो महानिधिः ॥८६॥
slokas.roman:
suvarṇabindur akṣobhyaḥ sarvavāgīśvareśvaraḥ,
mahāhrado mahāgarto mahābhūto mahānidhiḥ. (86)
slokas.text:
सुवर्णबिन्दुरक्षोभ्यः सर्ववागीश्वरेश्वरः ।
महाह्रदो महागर्तो महाभूतो महानिधिः ॥८६॥

suvarṇabindur akṣobhyaḥ sarvavāgīśvareśvaraḥ,
mahāhrado mahāgarto mahābhūto mahānidhiḥ. (86)

slokas.devanagari:
कुमुदः कुन्दरः कुन्दः पर्जन्यः पावनोऽनिलः ।
अमृताशोऽमृतवपुः सर्वज्ञः सर्वतोमुखः ॥८७॥
slokas.roman:
kumudaḥ kundaraḥ kundaḥ parjanyaḥ pāvano 'nilaḥ,
amṛtāśo 'mṛtavapuḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvatomukhaḥ. (87)
slokas.text:
कुमुदः कुन्दरः कुन्दः पर्जन्यः पावनोऽनिलः ।
अमृताशोऽमृतवपुः सर्वज्ञः सर्वतोमुखः ॥८७॥

kumudaḥ kundaraḥ kundaḥ parjanyaḥ pāvano 'nilaḥ,
amṛtāśo 'mṛtavapuḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvatomukhaḥ. (87)

slokas.devanagari:
सुलभः सुव्रतः सिद्धः शत्रुजिच्छत्रुतापनः ।
न्यग्रोधोऽदुम्बरोऽश्वत्थश्चाणूरान्ध्रनिषूदनः ॥८८॥
slokas.roman:
sulabhaḥ suvrataḥ siddhaḥ śatrujic chatrutāpanaḥ,
nyagrodho 'dumbaro 'śvatthaś cāṇūrāndhraniṣūdanaḥ. (88)
slokas.text:
सुलभः सुव्रतः सिद्धः शत्रुजिच्छत्रुतापनः ।
न्यग्रोधोऽदुम्बरोऽश्वत्थश्चाणूरान्ध्रनिषूदनः ॥८८॥

sulabhaḥ suvrataḥ siddhaḥ śatrujic chatrutāpanaḥ,
nyagrodho 'dumbaro 'śvatthaś cāṇūrāndhraniṣūdanaḥ. (88)

slokas.devanagari:
सहस्रार्चिः सप्तजिह्वः सप्तैधाः सप्तवाहनः ।
अमूर्तिरनघोऽचिन्त्यो भयकृद्भयनाशनः ॥८९॥
slokas.roman:
sahasrārciḥ saptajihvaḥ saptaidhāḥ saptavāhanaḥ,
amūrtir anagho 'cintyo bhayakṛd bhayanāśanaḥ. (89)
slokas.text:
सहस्रार्चिः सप्तजिह्वः सप्तैधाः सप्तवाहनः ।
अमूर्तिरनघोऽचिन्त्यो भयकृद्भयनाशनः ॥८९॥

sahasrārciḥ saptajihvaḥ saptaidhāḥ saptavāhanaḥ,
amūrtir anagho 'cintyo bhayakṛd bhayanāśanaḥ. (89)

slokas.devanagari:
अणुर्बृहत्कृशः स्थूलो गुणभृन्निर्गुणो महान् ।
अधृतः स्वधृतः स्वास्यः प्राग्वंशो वंशवर्धनः ॥९०॥
slokas.roman:
aṇur bṛhat kṛśaḥ sthūlo guṇabhṛn nirguṇo mahān,
adhṛtaḥ svadhṛtaḥ svāsyaḥ prāgvaṃśo vaṃśavardhanaḥ. (90)
slokas.text:
अणुर्बृहत्कृशः स्थूलो गुणभृन्निर्गुणो महान् ।
अधृतः स्वधृतः स्वास्यः प्राग्वंशो वंशवर्धनः ॥९०॥

aṇur bṛhat kṛśaḥ sthūlo guṇabhṛn nirguṇo mahān,
adhṛtaḥ svadhṛtaḥ svāsyaḥ prāgvaṃśo vaṃśavardhanaḥ. (90)

slokas.devanagari:
भारभृत् कथितो योगी योगीशः सर्वकामदः ।
आश्रमः श्रमणः क्षामः सुपर्णो वायुवाहनः ॥९१॥
slokas.roman:
bhārabhṛt kathito yogī yogīśaḥ sarvakāmadaḥ,
āśramaḥ śramaṇaḥ kṣāmaḥ suparṇo vāyuvāhanaḥ. (91)
slokas.text:
भारभृत् कथितो योगी योगीशः सर्वकामदः ।
आश्रमः श्रमणः क्षामः सुपर्णो वायुवाहनः ॥९१॥

bhārabhṛt kathito yogī yogīśaḥ sarvakāmadaḥ,
āśramaḥ śramaṇaḥ kṣāmaḥ suparṇo vāyuvāhanaḥ. (91)

slokas.devanagari:
धनुर्धरो धनुर्वेदो दण्डो दमयिता दमः ।
अपराजितः सर्वसहो नियन्ताऽनियमोऽयमः ॥९२॥
slokas.roman:
dhanurdharo dhanurvedo daṇḍo damayitā damaḥ,
aparājitaḥ sarvasaho niyantā 'niyamo 'yamaḥ. (92)
slokas.text:
धनुर्धरो धनुर्वेदो दण्डो दमयिता दमः ।
अपराजितः सर्वसहो नियन्ताऽनियमोऽयमः ॥९२॥

dhanurdharo dhanurvedo daṇḍo damayitā damaḥ,
aparājitaḥ sarvasaho niyantā 'niyamo 'yamaḥ. (92)

slokas.devanagari:
सत्त्ववान् सात्त्विकः सत्यः सत्यधर्मपरायणः ।
अभिप्रायः प्रियार्होऽर्हः प्रियकृत् प्रीतिवर्धनः ॥९३॥
slokas.roman:
sattvavān sāttvikaḥ satyaḥ satyadharmaparāyaṇaḥ,
abhiprāyaḥ priyārho 'rhaḥ priyakṛt prītivardhanāḥ. (93)
slokas.text:
सत्त्ववान् सात्त्विकः सत्यः सत्यधर्मपरायणः ।
अभिप्रायः प्रियार्होऽर्हः प्रियकृत् प्रीतिवर्धनः ॥९३॥

sattvavān sāttvikaḥ satyaḥ satyadharmaparāyaṇaḥ,
abhiprāyaḥ priyārho 'rhaḥ priyakṛt prītivardhanāḥ. (93)

slokas.devanagari:
विहायसगतिर्ज्योतिः सुरुचिर्हुतभुग्विभुः ।
रविर्विरोचनः सूर्यः सविता रविलोचनः ॥९४॥
slokas.roman:
vihāyasagatir jyotiḥ surucir hutabhug vibhuḥ,
ravir virocanaḥ sūryaḥ savitā ravilocanaḥ. (94)
slokas.text:
विहायसगतिर्ज्योतिः सुरुचिर्हुतभुग्विभुः ।
रविर्विरोचनः सूर्यः सविता रविलोचनः ॥९४॥

vihāyasagatir jyotiḥ surucir hutabhug vibhuḥ,
ravir virocanaḥ sūryaḥ savitā ravilocanaḥ. (94)

slokas.devanagari:
अनन्तो हुतभुग्भोक्ता सुखदो नैकजोऽग्रजः ।
अनिर्विण्णः सदामर्षी लोकाधिष्ठानमद्भुतः ॥९५॥
slokas.roman:
ananto hutabhug bhoktā sukhado naikajo 'grajaḥ,
anirviṇṇaḥ sadāmarṣī lokādhiṣṭhānam adbhutaḥ. (95)
slokas.text:
अनन्तो हुतभुग्भोक्ता सुखदो नैकजोऽग्रजः ।
अनिर्विण्णः सदामर्षी लोकाधिष्ठानमद्भुतः ॥९५॥

ananto hutabhug bhoktā sukhado naikajo 'grajaḥ,
anirviṇṇaḥ sadāmarṣī lokādhiṣṭhānam adbhutaḥ. (95)

slokas.devanagari:
सनात्सनातनतमः कपिलः कपिरव्ययः ।
स्वस्तिदः स्वस्तिकृत्स्वस्ति स्वस्तिभुक्स्वस्तिदक्षिणः ॥९६॥
slokas.roman:
sanāt sanātanatamaḥ kapilaḥ kapir avyayaḥ,
svastidaḥ svastikṛt svasti svastibhuk svastidakṣiṇaḥ. (96)
slokas.text:
सनात्सनातनतमः कपिलः कपिरव्ययः ।
स्वस्तिदः स्वस्तिकृत्स्वस्ति स्वस्तिभुक्स्वस्तिदक्षिणः ॥९६॥

sanāt sanātanatamaḥ kapilaḥ kapir avyayaḥ,
svastidaḥ svastikṛt svasti svastibhuk svastidakṣiṇaḥ. (96)

slokas.devanagari:
अरौद्रः कुण्डली चक्री विक्रम्यूर्जितशासनः ।
शब्दातिगः शब्दसहः शिशिरः शर्वरीकरः ॥९७॥
slokas.roman:
araudraḥ kuṇḍalī cakrī vikramī ūrjitaśāsanaḥ,
śabdātigaḥ śabdasahaḥ śiśiraḥ śarvarīkaraḥ. (97)
slokas.text:
अरौद्रः कुण्डली चक्री विक्रम्यूर्जितशासनः ।
शब्दातिगः शब्दसहः शिशिरः शर्वरीकरः ॥९७॥

araudraḥ kuṇḍalī cakrī vikramī ūrjitaśāsanaḥ,
śabdātigaḥ śabdasahaḥ śiśiraḥ śarvarīkaraḥ. (97)

slokas.devanagari:
अक्रूरः पेशलो दक्षो दक्षिणः क्षमिणांवरः ।
विद्वत्तमो वीतभयः पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तनः ॥९८॥
slokas.roman:
akrūraḥ peśalo dakṣo dakṣiṇaḥ kṣamiṇāṃ varaḥ,
vidvattamo vītabhayaḥ puṇyaśravaṇakīrtanaḥ. (98)
slokas.text:
अक्रूरः पेशलो दक्षो दक्षिणः क्षमिणांवरः ।
विद्वत्तमो वीतभयः पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तनः ॥९८॥

akrūraḥ peśalo dakṣo dakṣiṇaḥ kṣamiṇāṃ varaḥ,
vidvattamo vītabhayaḥ puṇyaśravaṇakīrtanaḥ. (98)

slokas.devanagari:
उत्तारणो दुष्कृतिहा पुण्यो दुःस्वप्ननाशनः ।
वीरहा रक्षणः सन्तो जीवनः पर्यवस्थितः ॥९९॥
slokas.roman:
uttāraṇo duṣkṛtihā puṇyo duḥsvapnanāśanaḥ,
vīrahā rakṣaṇaḥ santo jīvanaḥ paryavasthitaḥ. (99)
slokas.text:
उत्तारणो दुष्कृतिहा पुण्यो दुःस्वप्ननाशनः ।
वीरहा रक्षणः सन्तो जीवनः पर्यवस्थितः ॥९९॥

uttāraṇo duṣkṛtihā puṇyo duḥsvapnanāśanaḥ,
vīrahā rakṣaṇaḥ santo jīvanaḥ paryavasthitaḥ. (99)

slokas.devanagari:
अनन्तरूपोऽनन्तश्रीर्जितमन्युर्भयापहः ।
चतुरश्रो गभीरात्मा विदिशो व्यादिशो दिशः ॥१००॥
slokas.roman:
anantarūpo 'nantaśrīr jitamanyur bhayāpahaḥ,
caturaśro gabhīrātmā vidiśo vyādiśo diśaḥ. (100)
slokas.text:
अनन्तरूपोऽनन्तश्रीर्जितमन्युर्भयापहः ।
चतुरश्रो गभीरात्मा विदिशो व्यादिशो दिशः ॥१००॥

anantarūpo 'nantaśrīr jitamanyur bhayāpahaḥ,
caturaśro gabhīrātmā vidiśo vyādiśo diśaḥ. (100)

slokas.devanagari:
अनादिर्भूर्भुवो लक्ष्मीः सुवीरो रुचिराङ्गदः ।
जननो जनजन्मादिर्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥१०१॥
slokas.roman:
anādir bhūrbhuvo lakṣmīḥ suvīro rucirāṅgadaḥ,
janano janajanmādir bhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ. (101)
slokas.text:
अनादिर्भूर्भुवो लक्ष्मीः सुवीरो रुचिराङ्गदः ।
जननो जनजन्मादिर्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥१०१॥

anādir bhūrbhuvo lakṣmīḥ suvīro rucirāṅgadaḥ,
janano janajanmādir bhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ. (101)

slokas.devanagari:
आधारनिलयोऽधाता पुष्पहासः प्रजागरः ।
ऊर्ध्वगः सत्पथाचारः प्राणदः प्रणवः पणः ॥१०२॥
slokas.roman:
ādhāranilayo 'dhātā puṣpahāsaḥ prajāgaraḥ,
ūrdhvagaḥ satpathācāraḥ prāṇadaḥ praṇavaḥ paṇaḥ. (102)
slokas.text:
आधारनिलयोऽधाता पुष्पहासः प्रजागरः ।
ऊर्ध्वगः सत्पथाचारः प्राणदः प्रणवः पणः ॥१०२॥

ādhāranilayo 'dhātā puṣpahāsaḥ prajāgaraḥ,
ūrdhvagaḥ satpathācāraḥ prāṇadaḥ praṇavaḥ paṇaḥ. (102)

slokas.devanagari:
प्रमाणं प्राणनिलयः प्राणभृत्प्राणजीवनः ।
तत्त्वं तत्त्वविदेकात्मा जन्ममृत्युजरातिगः ॥१०३॥
slokas.roman:
pramāṇaṃ prāṇanilayaḥ prāṇabhṛt prāṇajīvanaḥ,
tattvaṃ tattvavid ekātmā janmamṛtyujarātigaḥ. (103)
slokas.text:
प्रमाणं प्राणनिलयः प्राणभृत्प्राणजीवनः ।
तत्त्वं तत्त्वविदेकात्मा जन्ममृत्युजरातिगः ॥१०३॥

pramāṇaṃ prāṇanilayaḥ prāṇabhṛt prāṇajīvanaḥ,
tattvaṃ tattvavid ekātmā janmamṛtyujarātigaḥ. (103)

slokas.devanagari:
भूर्भुवःस्वस्तरुस्तारः सविता प्रपितामहः ।
यज्ञो यज्ञपतिर्यज्वा यज्ञाङ्गो यज्ञवाहनः ॥१०४॥
slokas.roman:
bhūrbhuvaḥ svastaruḥ tāraḥ savitā prapitāmahaḥ,
yajño yajñapatir yajvā yajñāṅgo yajñavāhanaḥ. (104)
slokas.text:
भूर्भुवःस्वस्तरुस्तारः सविता प्रपितामहः ।
यज्ञो यज्ञपतिर्यज्वा यज्ञाङ्गो यज्ञवाहनः ॥१०४॥

bhūrbhuvaḥ svastaruḥ tāraḥ savitā prapitāmahaḥ,
yajño yajñapatir yajvā yajñāṅgo yajñavāhanaḥ. (104)

slokas.devanagari:
यज्ञभृद् यज्ञकृद् यज्ञी यज्ञभुग् यज्ञसाधनः ।
यज्ञान्तकृद् यज्ञगुह्यमन्नमन्नाद एव च ॥१०५॥
slokas.roman:
yajñabhṛd yajñakṛd yajñī yajñabhug yajñasādhanaḥ,
yajñāntakṛd yajñaguhyam annam annāda eva ca. (105)
slokas.text:
यज्ञभृद् यज्ञकृद् यज्ञी यज्ञभुग् यज्ञसाधनः ।
यज्ञान्तकृद् यज्ञगुह्यमन्नमन्नाद एव च ॥१०५॥

yajñabhṛd yajñakṛd yajñī yajñabhug yajñasādhanaḥ,
yajñāntakṛd yajñaguhyam annam annāda eva ca. (105)

slokas.devanagari:
आत्मयोनिः स्वयञ्जातो वैखानः सामगायनः ।
देवकीनन्दनः स्रष्टा क्षितीशः पापनाशनः ॥१०६॥
slokas.roman:
ātmayoniḥ svayaṃjāto vaikhānaḥ sāmagāyanaḥ,
devakīnandanaḥ sraṣṭā kṣitīśaḥ pāpanāśanaḥ. (106)
slokas.text:
आत्मयोनिः स्वयञ्जातो वैखानः सामगायनः ।
देवकीनन्दनः स्रष्टा क्षितीशः पापनाशनः ॥१०६॥

ātmayoniḥ svayaṃjāto vaikhānaḥ sāmagāyanaḥ,
devakīnandanaḥ sraṣṭā kṣitīśaḥ pāpanāśanaḥ. (106)

slokas.devanagari:
शङ्खभृन्नन्दकी चक्री शार्ङ्गधन्वा गदाधरः ।
रथाङ्गपाणिरक्षोभ्यः सर्वप्रहरणायुधः ॥१०७॥
सर्वप्रहरणायुध ॐ नम इति ।
slokas.roman:
śaṅkhabhṛn nandakī cakrī śārṅgadhanvā gadādharaḥ,
rathāṅgapāṇir akṣobhyaḥ sarvapraharaṇāyudhaḥ. (107)
slokas.text:
शङ्खभृन्नन्दकी चक्री शार्ङ्गधन्वा गदाधरः ।
रथाङ्गपाणिरक्षोभ्यः सर्वप्रहरणायुधः ॥१०७॥
सर्वप्रहरणायुध ॐ नम इति ।

śaṅkhabhṛn nandakī cakrī śārṅgadhanvā gadādharaḥ,
rathāṅgapāṇir akṣobhyaḥ sarvapraharaṇāyudhaḥ. (107)

slokas.devanagari:
वनमाली गदी शार्ङ्गी शङ्खी चक्री च नन्दकी ।
श्रीमान् नारायणो विष्णुर्वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॥१०८॥
श्री वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॐ नम इति ।
slokas.roman:
vanamālī gadī śārṅgī śaṅkhī cakrī ca nandakī,
śrīmān nārāyaṇo viṣṇur vāsudevo 'bhirakṣatu. (108)
slokas.text:
वनमाली गदी शार्ङ्गी शङ्खी चक्री च नन्दकी ।
श्रीमान् नारायणो विष्णुर्वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॥१०८॥
श्री वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॐ नम इति ।

vanamālī gadī śārṅgī śaṅkhī cakrī ca nandakī,
śrīmān nārāyaṇo viṣṇur vāsudevo 'bhirakṣatu. (108)

passages.text:
The Teaching of Viṣṇusahasranāma Based on
the teachings of Swami Dayananda Saraswati
       (With few Additions by Swamini
            Brahmaprajnananda)
Art work by Jamuna Inamdar

1

Important Note
This is the digital version of the book based on Pujya Swami Dayananda Saraswati's talks on
Viṣṇusahasranāma compiled by Smt Sunitha Santharam Amma and published by Sruti Seva trust, Arsha
Vidya Gurukulam, Coimbatore, South India, 2005.
This is to expressly state that this digital copy is meant for your personal use only and not for any
commercial use. This book or a part thereof should not be published in the form of a printed book without the
permission of Sruti Seva Trust, Coimbatore.
In the text, some additions have been made by Swamini Brahmaprajnananda in blue text which include some
sentences, relevant anecdotes or some stories from the Puranas.
With permission from the Trustees of Sruti Seva Trust 27th April 2020
26th April 2020
Namaste everyone.
On Akshaya Tritiya day in 2015 I had begun sharing Pujya Swami Dayananda ‘s teachings from the great
treasure that is in the form of Viṣṇu Sahasranāma.
For more than 2.5 years, the meaning of one word a day on a Facebook group and whatsapp group was shared
daily. This teaching was intended to be an online supplement to the study of the Bhagavad Gita for most of
my offline and online Vedanta students. But it became bi

group was shared
daily. This teaching was intended to be an online supplement to the study of the Bhagavad Gita for most of
my offline and online Vedanta students. But it became bigger than that. Many others joined and were able to
discover much joy and meaning in their lives with the discovery of Īśvara. For those who already chanted the
Viṣṇu Sahasranāma, their chanting gained greater depth and significance. Those who had never even heard
of it, were inspired to learn. Much puṇya was gained and shared.
It is only an abundance of blessings that on Akshaya Tritiya 2020 this digital copy based on the book is now
available for your personal use.
It is important to note that in the text I have added many sentences, some relevant anecdotes or some stories
from the Puranas as I found appropriate and these are in blue text. Shankara Bhashya (in Sanskrit) is not
included. If there are any errors found in this, I alone am responsible. Kindly bring it to my notice.
I am very grateful to Sruti Seva Trust, especially Smt Sunitha Santharam and Swami Sadatmananda ji to so
promptly and kindly give me permission to make this available online. As a result of their generosity, more
Vedanta students

y Smt Sunitha Santharam and Swami Sadatmananda ji to so
promptly and kindly give me permission to make this available online. As a result of their generosity, more
Vedanta students living outside India can benefit especially those who may find it difficult to source this
book.
My thanks to my loving and committed Vedanta students (Rashmi Kaikini, Rekha Nair and Sonali
Ambasankar) who keyed in the whole text.
Continuing sincere prayers to Bhagavan Viṣṇu for preserving our great parampara of self knowledge
transmitted down the ages and that true seekers may finally come home!
With love and prayers,
Swamini Brahmaprajnananda

Introduction
In our religious Indian culture, a person who constantly repeats the name of the Lord, would be looked upon
as a devotee. If the person keeps repeating His name the whole day, then that person is perhaps called ‘an
inspired devotee.’ But, when a person repeats the name of any given person the whole day, then, that person
is evidently, a mentally challenged person. Therefore, there is a difference in our attitude towards these two
persons. Nāma is the name and nām is the one, who has the name. If the name happens to be a time-bound
person with limitations, then repeating his name will become a mere obsession and will not be beneficial in
any manner. Repeating the name of a rich person and talking about his glories all the time, just because he
has a lot of power and influence, will not be be beneficial in any way. There is no benefit in repeating the
name of a time bound person. But when a person repeats the name of Parameśvara, the Lord, there is bound
to be a benefit, there is a purpose in it. Why should anyone repeat the names of the Lord? What are the
benefits? Why has the Lord so many names? What do they mean? Thus, these questions naturally arise!

Understanding The Word Īśvara
All these questions become meaningful, only when we understand this Lord or Īśvara. Who is Īśvara? The
Lord is called Īśvara. The word Īśvara is made up of two parts ‘ ś’ and vara.’ The part ’vara’ is called
‘pratyaya’ and the other part ‘ ś’ called ‘prakṛti,’ which is in the sense of ‘aiśvarye,’ ruling protection; just as
in the word ‘worker,’ ‘work ’ is ‘prakṛti’ and the ‘er’ is pratyaya.’ So Īśvara is the one who protects, sustains
and the one who is a source of blessing. In the world, we find that the work of a police commissioner is to
protect others. But we see that this person, whose job is to protect others, has also his own heavy security to
protect him. He also needs protection. An employer is one who is responsible for giving employment to a
large number of people and His employees look upon him. But we find that this employer also look up to a
number of people and also depends on a variety of things. People like doctors, who cure people of their
illnesses, also have to go to other doctors. Lawyers have to go to other lawyers and they also have to go to
doctors. Doctors also have to go to lawyers, to solve their problems. A person is called

ve to go to other doctors. Lawyers have to go to other lawyers and they also have to go to
doctors. Doctors also have to go to lawyers, to solve their problems. A person is called a prabhu, a master, a
lord. Still, when he wants to make a donation, he has to ask his wife, whether to donate or not. She may say
‘I will tell you tomorrow.’ Then, he has to wait. So everyone looks to someone else to make a decision.
Everyone depends on others; every master has other masters to turn to; every lord is lorded over by other
lords. Thus, no one seems to be a real prabhu, a real master. If this is the situation, who then is the real
prabhu? The real prabhu must be the one who does not have any other prabhu. He should be the one, who is
not dependent on anybody else, one who does not look up to anybody and one who is not lorded over by
anybody. In the Śiva-aṣṭottara, we find ‘Īśvarāya namaḥ’ which means my namas āra, salutations unto the
Lord. We also find another nāma as ‘an śvarāya namah.’ The word an śvara does not mean ‘one who is not a
Lord’ but means ‘the one who does not have a Lord.’ Īśvaraḥ na vidyate yasya’ He is the Īśvara and he has
no Īśvara to lord over him. Unto Him my salutatio

of one who always does, habitually does what is indicated by the verb. Thus added to the verbal root ‘ śaka

Sri Subramaniam is called in various ways as Subbu, Subbudu, Mani and also as S.S. For all these names,
one Subramaniam responds. He also responds to words of relation such as uncle, daddy, mamma, anna and
so on. All these are the different names for one and the same person. Now, does Īśvara also have similar

names? Yes. They are these thousand names and when one understands Īśvara, all these names become clear.
This Īśvara, who we say is the cause of this scheme of things called jagat, is to be discovered. We generally
assume, without much enquiry, that there is a power beyond ourselves called God. Organisations assume and
talk that there is a power and give it a location and gender as ‘Father in Heaven.’ He is presented as a God
who is highly judgemental and cannot stand any violation of His mandates etc. So much is taken for granted
that God is the most misunderstood person. Because of this, many great people like the mathematician-philosopher Bertrand Russell and others do not want to have anything to do with God or the theologies that
present Him. Some get away from such theologies while some others get along with these assumptions for
want of anything better. There are some others who ask, while I have enough to worry about in my life, why
should I bother with one more thing.

Cause for One’s Self-Disapproval
Every human being is a complex organism, highly self-conscious. Animals also have some self-consciousness in that they can recognize their own species, but they have neither complexes about non-
acceptance of themselves. They do not seem to want to be different from what they are, if their behavior
indicates anything. But the human being wants to be different in a hundred different ways, because he/she is
totally conscious of the self. This, therefore, leads to self-judgement and self-loathing which in turn gives rise to
complexes. So, the whole life is one of ‘becoming.’ The need to ‘become’ only reveals a self-disapproval and
a conclusion that one is limited in every way. We have a body-mind-sense-complex which has various
limitations. The body is limited time-wise, space-wise, object-wise; it is further limited from the standpoint
of colour, height, weight, appearance and so on. We are further limited health-wise, knowledge-wise,
money-wise, and so on. Therefore, in whichever way we look at ourselves, we are not acceptable to
ourselves. This self-disapproval and non-acceptance cannot be got rid of by mere positive thinking. If at all

t is happiness? Happiness is

many and varied. Things that are believed to exist are also many and varied. All these objects are objectified
by you the subject. But, when one is happy, does one relate to happiness as the subject for whom, the object

No Self-Acceptance Without Self-Discovery
The fact that ‘I am happy’ is known to me. This becomes the basis of all the disapprovals and struggles in
life, because, this is what one wants to be. I want to be happy. It is like a musician who sings in a concert.
You enjoy the music and then go up to congratulate the musician on his wonderful performance. But the
musician is apologetic and says ‘I did not do well.’ According to him he did not do well, but according to
you he did very well. He is unhappy with his performance because he knows his height, the octave that he
can normally reach, but could not reach that day. Therefore, he is not satisfied. Thus, there is a norm for each
one and this is the truth about our self also.
All our wants, judgements, complexes, anxieties, concerns and all other issues resolve in a moment of joy. It
does not wait for desires to get fulfilled. If all our desires have to be fulfilled to attain that moment to be
happy, then it will never be possible to be happy. There are different kinds of desires. There are desires
wherein one says,’ I wish I had done this.’ ‘I wish I had not done this!’ Thus there is always this guilt. ‘How

No Self-Discovery Without Discovering Īśvara
To now oneself, to understand all about oneself, one has to find one’s place in the scheme of things. You are
an individual in the scheme of things, which is the world. The world means the society, the country and
everything else that is immediately in front of you. This is the scheme in which one finds oneself. The
scheme of things implies that which is given. The body is given, the mind is given, senses are given,
contemporary society is given and you have to deal with all of them. The entire scheme of things is given
with a variety of possibilities. But even the possibilities are not known to you know. In the wake of better
data, you will discover more possibilities. It is all given. If all this is given, then naturally, there must be a

The World Is Intellegently Put Together
You are an individual, in the scheme of things called jagat, and this jagat is the total. The scheme of things,
called the jagat, is in time and space and it is given. It consists of all things – all situations, all forces, all
means and ends, all actions and reactions–all of which are interconnected. Your whole body-mind-sense-
complex is put together. In that, each part is also put together and they are all inter-connected. Even cells are
put together and every organ is also put together. The external world is also put together. The seed, the tree
and so on are all put together. The seed has enough nourishment to bring forth a tree. Thus, you can see that
it is not only put together but it is intelligently put together.

This Presupposes An Intelligent Creator
Anything intelligently put together to serve a purpose, is preceded by knowledge. Without that knowledge it
cannot be put together. The function is already visualized. For instance, what is the function of the kidney
cells? What is expected of each one of them? All this has been already visualized. Visualising implies
knowledge. Therefore, knowledge must precede anything that is intelligently put together and that
knowledge must necessarily abide in a conscious being. Whatever is intelligently put together is called
creation and there must necessarily be a conscious being behind every creation with the necessary
knowledge. The world that is the scheme of things, is created because it is intelligently put together. Soka
conscious being is assumed to be behind the entire created world. We understand that this being should have
existed, when the world was intelligently put together and it is still existent, because the intelligently put
together creation still continues to exist. This assumed intelligent conscious being, to account for the creation
of this world, should have the knowledge, because we are talking of the total world. The created j

s to exist. This assumed intelligent conscious being, to account for the creation
of this world, should have the knowledge, because we are talking of the total world. The created jagat itself
provides the clues to show that without a conscious being, nothing can be put together intelligently. For
instance, if there is a change that is taking place, say a bacterial change as in the case of milk turning into
curd, then, the creators are the bacteria and they are responsible for turning the milk into curd. Other kinds of
changes are also brought about by complex insects like termites, which make an anthill. To create this
anthill, which is intelligently put together, there is activity on the part of the termites. The honeybees make a
honeycomb. Any creation implies this kind of activity. The termites and the honeybees are intelligent beings
having the knowledge and the skill to build the anthill and the honeycomb. The human being is one who has
the capacity to think and develop the skills, and understand the capacities and possibilities that are given.
He/she is also given the capacity to create.

The Creator Of All Must Have All Knowledge And Skill
In this context, we are talking about the creation of all these possibilities, including the capacity to think and
create. Even the infrastructure for the scheme of things is created and put together. This entire jagat includes
one’s body, prāṇas, organs of perception and action and the thinking
faculty. The capacity to remember and recollect is all given. All this is included in the jagat, the total. So
individually and totally, everything is put together. The one who has the prior knowledge of this samagram
jagat, total world, is the one who has samagram jñānam, total knowledge. He has also the samagram v ryam,
the total skills to create. Such a being is called Bhagavān, Īśvara. This conscious being, Bhagavān, with total
knowledge and total skill, is unfortunately thought of as one who is sitting somewhere, in time and space.
But, time and space are not absolute. One cannot think of time and eternity together, because time itself is
created. Creation includes time, space and everything that is in it. The creator of this entire jagat, cannot sit in
time and space and therefore within the jagat and then create the jagat. To sit i

includes time, space and everything that is in it. The creator of this entire jagat, cannot sit in
time and space and therefore within the jagat and then create the jagat. To sit in the jagat and create the jagat
is absurd! So, the Lord, who has total knowledge and skill, cannot be located in the world. Then, can we say
that the Lord is located outside the jagat and from there He created the jagat? The concept ‘outside,’ is within

space alone. Bhagavān cannot be ‘outside’space, much less ‘inside’ space and time. Therefore, all that we
can say is that the very jagat including time and space is Bhagavān. This means that Bhagavān has to be
understood in a comprehensive way.

Īśvara Is Both The Maker And Material
Any creation requires two causes, the material cause and the efficient cause; the material and the maker. To
make a modaka, one should have the knowledge of making the modaka and also the material to make it.
Similarly, besides the knowledge to create, Īśvara must also have some material to create this world. Now,
where is this material? There is no material which is separate from Īśvara. If the material were to be separate
from Īśvara, then to separate the material, there should be space. Now, the question arises as to who created
that material and also who created the separating space? The Vedas are very clear. They say that everything
came from Īśvara. The Taittir ya-upaniṣad defines Īśvara thus, ‘yato vā imani bhūtāni jāyante, yena jātāni j
vanti, yat prayanti abhisamviśanti – that form which all these have come, in which all these exist, that is, by
which all these are sustained, and unto which all of them go back, that is, Īśvara. The material is Īśvara and
the space also is Īśvara. Let us for instance take a shirt. When we say shirt, we find that it is made of fabric.
Once the shirt is made, has the fabric gone and the shirt come? No. Sh

space also is Īśvara. Let us for instance take a shirt. When we say shirt, we find that it is made of fabric.
Once the shirt is made, has the fabric gone and the shirt come? No. Shirt and fabric both are there. Wherever
the shirt is, the fabric also is there. In fact, shirt is the fabric. Then why do you call it shirt? Because the shirt
serves a purpose – it can be worn. The shirt is therefore, neither satya, real like the fabric and at the same
time it is not tuccha, non-existent. It is mithyā, false. It is a real shirt, but it is not real like the fabric. The
weight of the shirt is the weight of the fabric. Therefore, fabric is satya, real, while the shirt is neither real nor
unreal. Shirt is only a nāma, a name with a corresponding rūpa, form of the fabric. Similarly, the whole jagat
is the names and forms of Bhagavān. That is why Bhagavān has many names and many forms.

Jagat Is Only The Names And Forms Of Bhagavān
In fact, all that is here is only names, and the names reveal Bhagavān. For example, fabric is only a name, the
truth of it being yarn. And yarn is another name, because it is nothing but
fibers. Fibers are but molecules, molecules are atoms and atoms are particles. Particles are mere concepts and
concepts are nothing but one’s consciousness. Knowledge is consciousness, but consciousness is not
knowledge. This knowledge alone manifests as the jagat. This total knowledge is the material cause and also
the maker. Īśvara, the Lord is both the maker and the material and hence the jagat is a manifestation of
Īśvara. Manifestation means, it is not separate from the material. It is an intelligent manifestation, like the
shirt which is a manifestation of the fabric, even though here an intelligent but separate tailor is involved,
unlike the intelligent manifestation of the jagat.
This manifestation of the creator is like one’s dream. In sleep there is no jagat. When you wake up partially,
you dream. In the dream the mind is awake partially, but at the same time, it is not awake to the body, to the

The Meaning Of The Word Bhagavān
The one who has bala, strength, is balavān. The one who has guna, good qualities, is gunavān and the one
who has dhana, wealth, is dhanavān. Similarly, the one who has bhaga is called Bhagavān. The word bhaga
is traditionally understood as the six-fold virtues in absolute measure. They are: jñāna – all knowledge;
vairāgya – total dispassion; v rya – all power; yaśas – all fame; śr – all wealth; and aiśvarya – overlordship.
These are known bhaga. The knowledge of Bhagavān is total and absolute, which means, it is free from any
ignorance. The one, who has total knowledge, does not require a mind, or the senses, or the means of
knowledge to know. When there is a mind, as in the case of an individual, ignorance would always be there.
Thus, whoever is endowed with a mind to know, cannot be Bhagavān. The individual jīva has only limited
knowledge and limited power. Absolute and total dispassion is called vairāgya, and the one who has this
dispassion has no longing or insecurity. But the individual has only limited vairāgya, like after eating a
banana, the peel is thrown away. There is no rāga or dveṣa, like or dislike towards the peel. Hence, there is

This is vairāgya. V rya is absolute power and this absolute power rests in Bhagavān. It is the capacity to
create, to sustain and to resolve, whereas, the individual jīva, has only limited power. All glories belong to
Him and they are absolute or samagram yaśas. That the eyes can see is a glory. That one can sing is a glory.
All these possibilities are certain capacities that people have and they are all nothing but the glories of Īśvara.
Every story, ever glory is the glory of Īśvara alone. Bhagavān has total wealth, śr. When all that is manifest
                                                        9

here is Īśvara, where is individual wealth? Our wealth is only what Bhagavān has given us. Children, home,
real estate, one’s capacity to earn, are all wealth and it is all given to us. It is the prasāda of Bhagavān, within
His infrastructure called the jagat and it does not belong to us. Total overlordship, aiśvarya, also belongs to
the Lord. Īśvarasya bhāva, the nature of the Lord, the capacity to lord over and to be in control is aiśvarya. A
plant, because of lack of water, is dying and you experience your aiśvarya, when you take care or do not take
care of it. Take for instance, an ant that has fallen into the water and is struggling for life. There is a chance,
to feel your aiśvarya, overlordship and help the ant to get out of the puddle. The same ant can be left in
the puddle, saying that it is its own karma. In case the same ant somehow escapes and gets into your ear, then
there is no more aiśvarya. That is why, we are not Īśvara. Īśvara has samagra aiśvarya. He is one, who is not
cause nor ruled by anyone, and who is not subject to the laws of someone else. These six absolute virtues
constitute bhaga and the one who has these six-fold bhaga is called Bhagavān. Īśvara is eve

y anyone, and who is not subject to the laws of someone else. These six absolute virtues
constitute bhaga and the one who has these six-fold bhaga is called Bhagavān. Īśvara is everything and
everything constitutes the various forms of Īśvara. When we say form, it can be further reduced to another
form and finally what is there, is only the cause of all, the Lord, and all that which was reduced to the Lord
were but names, sustained by the same lord. That is to say that a name is recognized with a form. All forms
are manifestations, as we have seen, of Īśvara. As the forms are countless, the names are also countless.
Thus, Īśvara has infinite names, though only a thousand are given here. Names are but words and certain
words, when understood, reveal Īśvara, like the word ‘pot’ reveals the object ‘pot.’ But we find that none of
the known words can really reveal Īśvara. Therefore, we have some special words which reveal the nature of
Īśvara and these words are three-fold - i. Words which reveal the essential nature of Īśvara, like satya,
meaning that which exists in all the three periods of time. ii. Words which reveal Īśvara, as the cause of the
jagat. All that is here is Īśvara, and

nature of Īśvara, like satya,
meaning that which exists in all the three periods of time. ii. Words which reveal Īśvara, as the cause of the
jagat. All that is here is Īśvara, and who is both the father and mother. Īśvara is ‘He’ from the stand-point of
the maker, the efficient cause and ‘She’ from the stand-point of the material cause, as the one who is
manifest in the form of the jagat. iii. Words which indicate the manifest form, as even the form of an avatāra.
Keeping māyā under His control, Īśvara is manifest in special forms like Śrī Rāma, Śrī Kṛṣṇa and so on. So
all these three sets of names given above constitute the sahasranāma, the thousand names of the Lord Viṣṇu.
The first two sets of names are common to every sahasranāma. Only the third set differs, in that, it has
special words for that particular form. Names like Viṣṇu, Śiva, Nārāyaṇa are all words that are used for
Parameśvara.
There are many sahasranāmas that are available, like the Lakṣm -sahasranāma, Śiva- sahasranāma, Gaṇeśa-
sahasranāma, Dakṣiṇāmūrti-sahasranāma, Subrahmaṇya-sahasranāma, and so on, but the most popular of
them are the Viṣṇu-sahasranama and the Lalita-sahasranāma. But, when merely the word sahas

ma, Dakṣiṇāmūrti-sahasranāma, Subrahmaṇya-sahasranāma, and so on, but the most popular of
them are the Viṣṇu-sahasranama and the Lalita-sahasranāma. But, when merely the word sahasranāma is
used, it refers to the Viṣṇu-sahasranāma which is the 149th adhyāya of the Anuśāsana-parva of the
Mahābhārata. Tradition holds that it was composed by Sanaka, and was given to Bh ṣma. Bh ṣma, in turn,
recited it to the Pāṇḍavas in the presence of Bhagavān Śrī Kṛṣṇa. At the end of the war, Yudhiṣṭhira, the
eldest of the Paṇḍavas, approached Bh ṣma who was lying on bed of arrows waiting for death, and asked him
‘who is the supreme Lord of all, who is the refuge of all and by praising and worshipping whom, one gains
all that is good and also the ultimate end?’ In answer to this question Bh ṣma gave the Viṣṇu- sahasranāma.
This is how the Mahabharata introduces the advent of these thousand names of the Lord.
The importance of the Viṣṇu-sahasranāma is further enhanced by the fact, that a person, no less than
Bhagavān Śr Sa karācārya had written an elaborate commentary, for each of the namas. The tradition goes,
that when Śr Sa arācārya wanted to write his first commentary on the Upaniṣads, he asked o

karācārya had written an elaborate commentary, for each of the namas. The tradition goes,
that when Śr Sa arācārya wanted to write his first commentary on the Upaniṣads, he asked one of his
disciples to bring the Taittir yopaniṣad, but the disciple brought the manuscripts of the Viṣṇu-sahasranāma.
This was repeated a second and a third time. Śr Sa arācārya then felt, that this was ordained by the Lord
himself and therefore he wrote his commentary on the Viṣṇu-sahasranāma first, before undertaking other
bhāṣya works. Śr Sa arācārya himself has written three invocatory ślokas, before commencing his
commentary. In the first verse, he salutes Śrī Kṛṣṇa as param brahma, ātmā and also as the guru. In the
second verse, he salutes Sage Vyāsa, the author of this sahasranāma and in the third verse, he praises the
sahasranāma itself. He also gives the reason for undertaking the explanation of these nāmas.

Om śuklāmbaradharam viṣṇum śaśivarṇam caturbhujam |
prasannavadanam dhyāyet sarvavighnopaśāntaye || 1 One should meditate on Him who wears a white dress,

any work. The Lord is invoked both as Vighnarāja and also as Vighnahara. Every action attracts two types of

whereas, the adṛṣṭa-phala is enjoyed much later, either in this birth or in other birth. A human being is
endowed with a free will. That free will can be used to do what is right, dharma. Then, the person incurs an
adṛṣṭa-phala called puṇya which in turn manifests as circumstances which will give sukha. Or, the free will
can be abused and used to hurt other living beings or other human beings and go against what is right,

an be abused and used to hurt other living beings or other human beings and go against what is right,

duḥkha. So, the puṇya and pāpa stand in the account of the jīva, the individual, as long as one looks upon
himself as a limited person, as a doer and enjoyer. In accordance to one’s karma, a person is born at a
particular place and time, with a particular parentage and culture. All the karma-phala standing in one’s name

But the law of means and ends, is presided over by Īśvara and with reference to that, the Lord is called
karmādhya ṣa and as the giver of the fruits of action, He is called karma-phala-dātā.
The Lord is called Vighnarājā, the one who presides over the obstacles ‘vighnānām rājā.’ He is the one who

asking Him to remove the obstacles. This is a well-directed prayer. Īśvara is invoked not only as Vighnarāja,

irritate you) and iii. ādhidaivika–obstacles caused by natural forces like earthquakes, storms

wing few verses are in the form of a

Veda-vyāsa himself, was telling this story to anamejaya, the son of King ar kṣit, when he was doing a sarpa-
yāga. Thus, this is a dialogue between Ugraśravas and Sage Śaunaka in which there is a dialogue between
Vaiśampāyana and
Janamejaya. Vaiśampāyana now brings out the dialogue between Yudhiṣṭhira and Bh ṣma, which is relevant

ow brings out the dialogue between Yudhiṣṭhira and Bh ṣma, which is relevant

ime am daivatam loke im vā’pye am parāyaṇam | stuvantaḥ am amarcantaḥ prāpnuyurmānavāḥ śubham || 7
Yudhiṣṭhira said: What is that one Lord spoken of in all the śāstras? Which is the ultimate goal? Knowing
and worshipping whom, human beings would gain auspiciousness?
Yudhiṣṭhira asks Bh ṣma the following six questions. For all the questions Bh ṣma points out to Nārāyaṇa as
the one answer and then gives out the thousand names of the Lord. Even though tradition makes it out as six
separate questions, all of them are interconnected. His first question is, loke im e am daivatam–what is that
one devatā, one Lord, that is spoken of in all the śāstras, by whose order alone all the other devatās function?
Here, the word loka is derived as l

e im e am daivatam–what is that
one devatā, one Lord, that is spoken of in all the śāstras, by whose order alone all the other devatās function?
Here, the word loka is derived as lokyate anena–that by which one gains knowledge. Thus it refers to the
vidyā- sthānas, the śāstras, which are the sources of knowledge. Loka kim ekam parāyanam–What is that
param ayanam, that ultimate end mentioned in all the śāstras? Which is prāpyam sthānam, that one end to be
accomplished by all, and gaining which there is no other end to be accomplished? What is that end, knowing
which, bhidyate hṛdaya- granthiḥ–the knots of the heart are broken, chidyante sarva-samśayā–all the doubts
are resolved, kṣ yante ca asya karmāṇi–one’s karmas are all destroyed and there is no more rebirth?
(Mu.Up.2.2.8). What is that by knowing which, mokṣa which is characterized by limitless anandā is gained
and there is no more fear from anything else, because there is no second thing? What is that entering which
there is no coming back and knowing which one becomes that itself? What is that leaving which there is no
other path for human being? That can only be one thing, Brahman, because the upaniṣad says, ‘brahmaveda
brahm

which one becomes that itself? What is that leaving which there is no
other path for human being? That can only be one thing, Brahman, because the upaniṣad says, ‘brahmaveda
brahmaiva bhavati–the knower of Brahman becomes Brahman itself’(Mu.Up.3.2.9). Thus the ultimate end in
life is asked as the second question. Tell me that one Lord, knowing and understanding whom, stuvantaḥ–

the highest means, by which one can
